import qualified HsLua as Lua

import qualified Data.Aeson.KeyMap as KeyMap
import qualified Data.Aeson.Key as AesonKey

-- | Register the json module in the Lua state.
registerJsonModule :: Lua.State -> IO ()
registerJsonModule lstate = Lua.runWith lstate $ do
    Lua.newtable

    Lua.pushName "encode"
    Lua.pushHaskellFunction luaEncode
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

    Lua.pushName "decode"
    Lua.pushHaskellFunction luaDecode
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

    Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "json")

-- | Encode a Lua value to JSON string.
luaEncode :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaEncode = do
    jsonVal <- luaToAesonValue
    let txt = LazyText.toStrict $ AesonText.encodeToLazyText jsonVal
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 txt)
    pure 1

-- | Decode a JSON string to a Lua value.
luaDecode :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaDecode = do
    str <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 1
    case Aeson.eitherDecodeStrict' str of
        Left err -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack err)
            pure 2
        Right val -> do
            aesonToLuaValue val
            pure 1

-- | Convert Lua value at top of stack to Aeson Value.
luaToAesonValue :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
luaToAesonValue = do
    ltype <- Lua.ltype (Lua.nthTop 1)
    case ltype of
        Lua.TypeNil -> do
            Lua.pop 1
            pure Aeson.Null
        Lua.TypeBoolean -> do
            b <- Lua.toboolean (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.Bool b
        Lua.TypeNumber -> do
            mNum <- Lua.tonumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            case mNum of
                Nothing -> pure Aeson.Null
                Just n -> pure $ Aeson.Number $ fromRealFloat n
        Lua.TypeString -> do
            s <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.String (Text.decodeUtf8 s)
        Lua.TypeTable -> do
            convertTable
        _ -> do
            str <- luaValueToString
            pure $ Aeson.String str
  where
    luaValueToString :: Lua.LuaE e Text
    luaValueToString = do
        Lua.pushglobaltable
        Lua.pushName (Lua.Name "tostring")
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)
        _ <- Lua.pcall 1 1 Nothing
        s <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
        Lua.pop 1
        pure $ Text.decodeUtf8 s

convertTable :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertTable = do
    isArr <- isArray
    if isArr
        then convertArray
        else convertObject

isArray :: Lua.LuaE e Bool
isArray = do
    Lua.pushnil
    isSequential 1
  where
    isSequential expectedIdx = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then pure True
            else do
                isNum <- Lua.isnumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
                if isNum
                    then do
                        mIdx <- Lua.tointeger (Lua.nthTop 1)
                        Lua.pop 2
                        case mIdx of
                            Just idx
                                | idx == fromIntegral expectedIdx ->
                                    isSequential (expectedIdx + 1)
                            _ -> pure False
                    else do
                        Lua.pop 2
                        pure False

convertArray :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertArray = do
    vals <- collectArrayValues
    pure $ Aeson.Array (Vector.fromList vals)

collectArrayValues :: Lua.LuaE e [Aeson.Value]
collectArrayValues = do
    len' <- Lua.rawlen (Lua.nthTop 1)
    vals <- mapM (\i -> do
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        val <- luaToAesonValue
        pure val
        ) [1 .. fromIntegral len']
    Lua.pop 1
    pure vals

convertObject :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertObject = do
    pairs <- collectObjectPairs
    pure $ Aeson.Object (KeyMap.fromList pairs)

collectObjectPairs :: Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
collectObjectPairs = do
    Lua.pushnil
    go []
  where
    go acc = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then do
                Lua.pop 1
                pure acc
            else do
                key <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
                val <- luaToAesonValue
                Lua.pop 1
                go ((AesonKey.fromText (Text.decodeUtf8 key), val) : acc)

aesonToLuaValue :: Aeson.Value -> Lua.LuaE e ()
aesonToLuaValue Aeson.Null = Lua.pushnil
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Bool b) = Lua.pushboolean b
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Number n) = 
    case floatingOrInteger n of
        Left d -> Lua.pushnumber d
        Right i -> Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.String t) = Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 t)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Array arr) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(i, val) -> do
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral (i + 1))
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (zip [0..] (Vector.toList arr))
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Object obj) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(k, val) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ AesonKey.toText k)
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (KeyMap.toList obj)
"}

function.arguments:
{"filepath": "./src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox/Modules/Http.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}
{-# LANGUAGE ScopedTypeVariables #-}

{- | HTTP client module for LuaToolbox.
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Http (
    HttpConfig (..),
    registerHttpModule,
) where

import qualified Data.ByteString as BS
import Control.Exception (try, SomeException)
import Control.Monad.IO.Class (liftIO)
import Data.ByteString (ByteString)
import qualified Data.ByteString.Lazy as LBS
import Data.Maybe (fromMaybe)
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified HsLua as Lua
import Network.HTTP.Client (
    Manager,
    Request (..),
    RequestBody (RequestBodyLBS, RequestBodyBS),
    Response (..),
    httpLbs,
    parseRequest,
 )
import qualified Network.HTTP.Client.TLS as HTTPS
import Network.HTTP.Types (Status, statusCode)
import qualified Network.HTTP.Types.Header as Header

-- | HTTP module configuration.
data HttpConfig = HttpConfig
    { httpAllowedHosts :: [Text]
    }
    deriving (Show, Eq)

-- | Register the http module.
registerHttpModule :: Lua.State -> HttpConfig -> IO ()
registerHttpModule lstate config = do
    manager <- HTTPS.newTlsManager
    Lua.runWith lstate $ do
        Lua.newtable

        addFunction "get" (luaGet config manager)
        addFunction "post" (luaPost config manager)
        addFunction "request" (luaRequest config manager)

        Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "http")
  where
    addFunction name fn = do
        Lua.pushName name
        Lua.pushHaskellFunction fn
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

-- | HTTP GET request.
luaGet :: HttpConfig -> Manager -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaGet config manager = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop
    if nargs < 1
        then do
            Lua.pop (fromIntegral nargs)
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring "Usage: http.get(url, [options])"
            pure 2
        else do
            urlBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            mOptions <- if nargs >= 2
                then do
                    ltype <- Lua.ltype (Lua.nthTop 2)
                    if ltype == Lua.TypeTable
                        then parseOptions (Lua.nthTop 2)
                        else pure (Just defaultOptions)
                else pure (Just defaultOptions)
            Lua.pop (fromIntegral nargs)
            
            let url = Text.unpack $ Text.decodeUtf8 urlBs
            case validateUrl config url of
                Left err -> do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
                    pure 2
                Right validUrl -> do
                    let opts = fromMaybe defaultOptions mOptions
                    performRequest manager "GET" validUrl opts (RequestBodyLBS LBS.empty)

-- | HTTP POST request.
luaPost :: HttpConfig -> Manager -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaPost config manager = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop
    if nargs < 2
        then do
            Lua.pop (fromIntegral nargs)
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring "Usage: http.post(url, body, [options])"
            pure 2
        else do
            urlBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
            bodyBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            mOptions <- if nargs >= 3
                then do
                    ltype <- Lua.ltype (Lua.nthTop 3)
                    if ltype == Lua.TypeTable
                        then parseOptions (Lua.nthTop 3)
                        else pure (Just defaultOptions)
                else pure (Just defaultOptions)
            Lua.pop (fromIntegral nargs)
            
            let url = Text.unpack $ Text.decodeUtf8 urlBs
            case validateUrl config url of
                Left err -> do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
                    pure 2
                Right validUrl -> do
                    let opts = fromMaybe defaultOptions mOptions
                    performRequest manager "POST" validUrl opts (RequestBodyBS bodyBs)

-- | Generic HTTP request.
luaRequest :: HttpConfig -> Manager -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaRequest config manager = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop
    if nargs < 1
        then do
            Lua.pop (fromIntegral nargs)
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring "Usage: http.request(options)"
            pure 2
        else do
            optsIdx <- Lua.nthTop 1
            
            Lua.pushstring "method"
            _ <- Lua.gettable optsIdx
            mMethodBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            
            Lua.pushstring "url"
            _ <- Lua.gettable optsIdx
            urlBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            
            Lua.pushstring "body"
            _ <- Lua.gettable optsIdx
            ltype <- Lua.ltype (Lua.nthTop 1)
            mBodyBs <- if ltype == Lua.TypeString
                then do
                    bs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
                    pure (Just bs)
                else pure Nothing
            Lua.pop 1
            
            mOptions <- parseOptions optsIdx
            
            Lua.pop (fromIntegral nargs)
            
            let url = Text.unpack $ Text.decodeUtf8 urlBs
            case validateUrl config url of
                Left err -> do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
                    pure 2
                Right validUrl -> do
                    let opts = fromMaybe defaultOptions mOptions
                    let reqMethod = if BS.null mMethodBs then "GET" else Text.decodeUtf8 mMethodBs
                    let reqBody = case mBodyBs of
                            Just bs -> RequestBodyBS bs
                            Nothing -> RequestBodyLBS LBS.empty
                    performRequest manager (Text.unpack reqMethod) validUrl opts reqBody

-- | Request options.
data RequestOptions = RequestOptions
    { optHeaders :: [(ByteString, ByteString)]
    }

defaultOptions :: RequestOptions
defaultOptions = RequestOptions []

-- | Parse options table.
parseOptions :: Lua.StackIndex -> Lua.LuaE e (Maybe RequestOptions)
parseOptions idx = do
    ltype <- Lua.ltype idx
    if ltype /= Lua.TypeTable
        then pure Nothing
        else do
            Lua.pushnil
            headers <- parseHeaders idx []
            pure $ Just $ RequestOptions headers
  where
    parseHeaders tableIdx acc = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop $ stackIdxToInt tableIdx + 1)
        if not hasNext
            then pure acc
            else do
                keyBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
                Lua.pop 1
                if keyBs == "headers"
                    then do
                        ltype <- Lua.ltype (Lua.nthTop 1)
                        if ltype == Lua.TypeTable
                            then do
                                hdrs <- parseHeadersTable (Lua.nthTop 1)
                                parseHeaders tableIdx (hdrs ++ acc)
                            else parseHeaders tableIdx acc
                    else parseHeaders tableIdx acc

    parseHeadersTable idx' = do
        Lua.pushnil
        go []
      where
        go acc = do
            hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop $ stackIdxToInt idx' + 1)
            if not hasNext
                then pure acc
                else do
                    keyBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
                    valBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
                    Lua.pop 1
                    go ((keyBs, valBs) : acc)

stackIdxToInt :: Lua.StackIndex -> Int
stackIdxToInt (Lua.StackIndex n) = fromIntegral n

-- | Validate URL.
validateUrl :: HttpConfig -> String -> Either Text String
validateUrl config url
    | null (httpAllowedHosts config) = Left "No hosts allowed"
    | otherwise = 
        case extractHost url of
            Nothing -> Left "Could not extract host from URL"
            Just host -> 
                if host `elem` httpAllowedHosts config
                    then Right url
                    else Left $ "Host not allowed: " <> host

-- | Extract host from URL.
extractHost :: String -> Maybe Text
extractHost url = 
    let url' = if "://" `Text.isInfixOf` Text.pack url
               then drop 1 $ dropWhile (/= '/') $ dropWhile (/= ':') $ dropWhile (/= '/') url
               else url
        hostPort = takeWhile (/= '/') url'
        host = takeWhile (/= ':') hostPort
    in if null host then Nothing else Just (Text.pack host)

-- | Perform HTTP request.
performRequest :: Manager -> String -> String -> RequestOptions -> RequestBody -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
performRequest manager reqMethod url opts reqBody = do
    result <- liftIO $ try $ do
        initReq <- parseRequest url
        let req = initReq
                { method = Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack reqMethod
                , requestBody = reqBody
                , requestHeaders = map (\(k, v) -> (Header.hCustom k, v)) (optHeaders opts)
                }
        httpLbs req manager
    
    case result of
        Left (e :: SomeException) -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
            pure 2
        Right response -> do
            pushResponse response
            pure 1

-- | Push response as Lua table.
pushResponse :: Response LBS.ByteString -> Lua.LuaE e ()
pushResponse response = do
    Lua.newtable
    
    Lua.pushstring "status"
    Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral $ statusCode $ responseStatus response)
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
    
    Lua.pushstring "headers"
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(name, val) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.toLower $ Text.pack $ show name)
        Lua.pushstring val
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (responseHeaders response)
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
    
    Lua.pushstring "body"
    Lua.pushstring (LBS.toStrict $ responseBody response)
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
"}

import qualified Data.ByteString as BS
import Control.Exception (try, SomeException)
import Control.Monad.IO.Class (liftIO)
import Data.ByteString (ByteString)
import qualified Data.ByteString.Lazy as LBS

import qualified Data.Text as Text

    Manager,

 )

-- | HTTP module configuration.
data HttpConfig = HttpConfig
    { httpAllowedHosts :: [Text]
    }
    deriving (Show, Eq)

-- | Register the http module.
registerHttpModule :: Lua.State -> HttpConfig -> IO ()
registerHttpModule lstate config = do
    manager <- HTTPS.newTlsManager
    Lua.runWith lstate $ do
        Lua.newtable

        addFunction "get" (luaGet config manager)
        addFunction "post" (luaPost config manager)
        addFunction "request" (luaRequest config manager)

        Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "http")
  where
    addFunction name fn = do
        Lua.pushName name
        Lua.pushHaskellFunction fn
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

-- | HTTP GET request.
luaGet :: HttpConfig -> Manager -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaGet config manager = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop
    if nargs < 1
        then do
            Lua.pop (fromIntegral nargs)
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring "Usage: http.get(url, [options])"
            pure 2
        else do
            urlBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            mOptions <- if nargs >= 2
                then do
                    ltype <- Lua.ltype (Lua.nthTop 2)
                    if ltype == Lua.TypeTable
                        then parseOptions (Lua.nthTop 2)
                        else pure (Just defaultOptions)
                else pure (Just defaultOptions)
            Lua.pop (fromIntegral nargs)
            
            let url = Text.unpack $ Text.decodeUtf8 urlBs
            case validateUrl config url of
                Left err -> do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
                    pure 2
                Right validUrl -> do
                    let opts = fromMaybe defaultOptions mOptions
                    performRequest manager "GET" validUrl opts (RequestBodyLBS LBS.empty)

-- | HTTP POST request.
luaPost :: HttpConfig -> Manager -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaPost config manager = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop
    if nargs < 2
        then do
            Lua.pop (fromIntegral nargs)
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring "Usage: http.post(url, body, [options])"
            pure 2
        else do
            urlBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
            bodyBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            mOptions <- if nargs >= 3
                then do
                    ltype <- Lua.ltype (Lua.nthTop 3)
                    if ltype == Lua.TypeTable
                        then parseOptions (Lua.nthTop 3)
                        else pure (Just defaultOptions)
                else pure (Just defaultOptions)
            Lua.pop (fromIntegral nargs)
            
            let url = Text.unpack $ Text.decodeUtf8 urlBs
            case validateUrl config url of
                Left err -> do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
                    pure 2
                Right validUrl -> do
                    let opts = fromMaybe defaultOptions mOptions
                    performRequest manager "POST" validUrl opts (RequestBodyBS bodyBs)

-- | Generic HTTP request.
luaRequest :: HttpConfig -> Manager -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaRequest config manager = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop
    if nargs < 1
        then do
            Lua.pop (fromIntegral nargs)
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring "Usage: http.request(options)"
            pure 2
        else do
            optsIdx <- Lua.nthTop 1
            
            Lua.pushstring "method"
            _ <- Lua.gettable optsIdx
            mMethodBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            
            Lua.pushstring "url"
            _ <- Lua.gettable optsIdx
            urlBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            
            Lua.pushstring "body"
            _ <- Lua.gettable optsIdx
            ltype <- Lua.ltype (Lua.nthTop 1)
            mBodyBs <- if ltype == Lua.TypeString
                then do
                    bs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
                    pure (Just bs)
                else pure Nothing
            Lua.pop 1
            
            mOptions <- parseOptions optsIdx
            
            Lua.pop (fromIntegral nargs)
            
            let url = Text.unpack $ Text.decodeUtf8 urlBs
            case validateUrl config url of
                Left err -> do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
                    pure 2
                Right validUrl -> do
                    let opts = fromMaybe defaultOptions mOptions
                    let reqMethod = if BS.null mMethodBs then "GET" else Text.decodeUtf8 mMethodBs
                    let reqBody = case mBodyBs of
                            Just bs -> RequestBodyBS bs
                            Nothing -> RequestBodyLBS LBS.empty
                    performRequest manager (Text.unpack reqMethod) validUrl opts reqBody

-- | Request options.
data RequestOptions = RequestOptions
    { optHeaders :: [(ByteString, ByteString)]
    }

defaultOptions :: RequestOptions
defaultOptions = RequestOptions []

-- | Parse options table.
parseOptions :: Lua.StackIndex -> Lua.LuaE e (Maybe RequestOptions)
parseOptions idx = do
    ltype <- Lua.ltype idx
    if ltype /= Lua.TypeTable
        then pure Nothing
        else do
            Lua.pushnil
            headers <- parseHeaders idx []
            pure $ Just $ RequestOptions headers
  where
    parseHeaders tableIdx acc = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop $ stackIdxToInt tableIdx + 1)
        if not hasNext
            then pure acc
            else do
                keyBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
                Lua.pop 1
                if keyBs == "headers"
                    then do
                        ltype <- Lua.ltype (Lua.nthTop 1)
                        if ltype == Lua.TypeTable
                            then do
                                hdrs <- parseHeadersTable (Lua.nthTop 1)
                                parseHeaders tableIdx (hdrs ++ acc)
                            else parseHeaders tableIdx acc
                    else parseHeaders tableIdx acc

    parseHeadersTable idx' = do
        Lua.pushnil
        go []
      where

            hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop $ stackIdxToInt idx' + 1)
            if not hasNext
                then pure acc
                else do
                    keyBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
                    valBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
                    Lua.pop 1
                    go ((keyBs, valBs) : acc)

stackIdxToInt :: Lua.StackIndex -> Int
stackIdxToInt (Lua.StackIndex n) = fromIntegral n

validateUrl :: HttpConfig -> String -> Either Text String
validateUrl config url
    | null (httpAllowedHosts config) = Left "No hosts allowed"
    | otherwise = 
        case extractHost url of
            Nothing -> Left "Could not extract host from URL"
            Just host -> 
                if host `elem` httpAllowedHosts config
                    then Right url
                    else Left $ "Host not allowed: " <> host

-- | Extract host from URL.
extractHost :: String -> Maybe Text
extractHost url = 
    let url' = if "://" `Text.isInfixOf` Text.pack url
               then drop 1 $ dropWhile (/= '/') $ dropWhile (/= ':') $ dropWhile (/= '/') url
               else url
        hostPort = takeWhile (/= '/') url'
        host = takeWhile (/= ':') hostPort
    in if null host then Nothing else Just (Text.pack host)

-- | Perform HTTP request.
performRequest :: Manager -> String -> String -> RequestOptions -> RequestBody -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
performRequest manager reqMethod url opts reqBody = do
    result <- liftIO $ try $ do
        initReq <- parseRequest url
        let req = initReq
                { method = Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack reqMethod
                , requestBody = reqBody
                , requestHeaders = map (\(k, v) -> (Header.hCustom k, v)) (optHeaders opts)
                }
        httpLbs req manager
    
    case result of
        Left (e :: SomeException) -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
            pure 2
        Right response -> do
            pushResponse response
            pure 1

-- | Push response as Lua table.
pushResponse :: Response LBS.ByteString -> Lua.LuaE e ()
pushResponse response = do
    Lua.newtable
    
    Lua.pushstring "status"
    Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral $ statusCode $ responseStatus response)
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
    
    Lua.pushstring "headers"
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(name, val) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.toLower $ Text.pack $ show name)
        Lua.pushstring val
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (responseHeaders response)
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
    
    Lua.pushstring "body"
    Lua.pushstring (LBS.toStrict $ responseBody response)
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

You are an expert Haskell and Bash programmer specialized in writing clean, idiomatic, and efficient Haskell code.
When writing Bash code:
- Avoid bash-specific logic but bash-ism are fine.
- Document required shell tools at the top of the bash script.
When writing Haskell code:
- Use modern Haskell practices (base >= 4.14)
- Prefer pure functions and explicit type signatures
- Use standard libraries when appropriate
- Add clear comments for complex logic
- Follow consistent formatting
Use list-files to explore the project structure, read-file to understand existing code, write-file to create or modify files, and check-code to verify your work.
Always compile code before considering the job done.
Your output format may be stored as commit message: hence the first line of your last message should be a summary line.

{-# LANGUAGE ScopedTypeVariables #-}

{- | Sandboxed filesystem module for LuaToolbox.
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Fs (
    FsConfig (..),
    registerFsModule,
) where

import qualified Data.ByteString as BS
import Control.Exception (try, IOException)
import Control.Monad.IO.Class (liftIO)
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified HsLua as Lua
import System.Directory (
    createDirectoryIfMissing,
    doesDirectoryExist,
    doesFileExist,
    listDirectory,
 )
import System.FilePath (isAbsolute, normalise, (</>), takeDirectory, isPathSeparator)
import Data.List (isPrefixOf)

-- | Filesystem module configuration with path sandboxing.
data FsConfig = FsConfig
    { fsAllowedPaths :: [FilePath]
    }
    deriving (Show, Eq)

-- | Register the fs module in the Lua state.
registerFsModule :: Lua.State -> FsConfig -> IO ()
registerFsModule lstate config = Lua.runWith lstate $ do
    Lua.newtable

    addFunction "read" (luaRead config)
    addFunction "write" (luaWrite config)
    addFunction "exists" (luaExists config)
    addFunction "list" (luaList config)
    addFunction "mkdir" (luaMkdir config)
    addFunction "isdir" (luaIsDir config)
    addFunction "isfile" (luaIsFile config)

    Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "fs")
  where
    addFunction name fn = do
        Lua.pushName name
        Lua.pushHaskellFunction fn
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

-- | Read file contents.
luaRead :: FsConfig -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaRead config = do
    mPath <- getPathArg config 1
    case mPath of
        Left err -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)

luaWrite :: FsConfig -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaWrite config = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop
    if nargs < 2
        then do
            Lua.pop (fromIntegral nargs)
            Lua.pushboolean False
            Lua.pushstring "Usage: fs.write(path, content)"
            pure 2
        else do
            mPath <- getPathArg config (nargs - 1)
            content <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop (fromIntegral nargs)
            case mPath of
                Left err -> do
                    Lua.pushboolean False
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
                    pure 2
                Right validPath -> do
                    let parentDir = takeDirectory validPath
                    parentResult <- liftIO $ try $ createDirectoryIfMissing True parentDir
                    case parentResult of
                        Left (e :: IOException) -> do
                            Lua.pushboolean False
                            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
                            pure 2
                        Right () -> do
                            result <- liftIO $ try $ BS.writeFile validPath content
                            case result of
                                Left (e :: IOException) -> do
                                    Lua.pushboolean False
                                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
                                    pure 2
                                Right () -> do
                                    Lua.pushboolean True
                                    pure 1

luaExists :: FsConfig -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaExists config = do
    mPath <- getPathArg config 1
    case mPath of
        Left _ -> do
            Lua.pushboolean False
            pure 1
        Right validPath -> do
            fileExists <- liftIO $ doesFileExist validPath
            dirExists <- liftIO $ doesDirectoryExist validPath
            Lua.pushboolean (fileExists || dirExists)
            pure 1

-- | List directory contents.
luaList :: FsConfig -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaList config = do
    mPath <- getPathArg config 1
    case mPath of
        Left err -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
            pure 2
        Right validPath -> do
            isDir <- liftIO $ doesDirectoryExist validPath
            if not isDir
                then do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    Lua.pushstring "Not a directory"
                    pure 2
                else do
                    result <- liftIO $ try $ listDirectory validPath
                    case result of
                        Left (e :: IOException) -> do
                            Lua.pushnil
                            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
                            pure 2

                    pure 1

            pure 1

You are an expert Haskell and Bash programmer specialized in writing clean, idiomatic, and efficient Haskell code.
When writing Bash code:
- Avoid bash-specific logic but bash-ism are fine.
- Document required shell tools at the top of the bash script.
When writing Haskell code:
- Use modern Haskell practices (base >= 4.14)
- Prefer pure functions and explicit type signatures
- Use standard libraries when appropriate
- Add clear comments for complex logic
- Follow consistent formatting
Use list-files to explore the project structure, read-file to understand existing code, write-file to create or modify files, and check-code to verify your work.
Always compile code before considering the job done.
Your output format may be stored as commit message: hence the first line of your last message should be a summary line.

{"filepath": "./src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox/Modules/Time.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}
{-# LANGUAGE ScopedTypeVariables #-}

{- | Time module for LuaToolbox.
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Time (
    registerTimeModule,
) where

import Control.Concurrent (threadDelay)
import Control.Monad.IO.Class (liftIO)
import Data.Time (UTCTime, defaultTimeLocale, formatTime, getCurrentTime, diffUTCTime)
import Data.Time.Clock.POSIX (utcTimeToPOSIXSeconds, posixSecondsToUTCTime)
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified HsLua as Lua

-- | Register the time module in the Lua state.
registerTimeModule :: Lua.State -> IO ()
registerTimeModule lstate = Lua.runWith lstate $ do
    Lua.newtable

    addFunction "now" luaNow
    addFunction "sleep" luaSleep
    addFunction "format" luaFormat
    addFunction "diff" luaDiff

    Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "time")
  where
    addFunction name fn = do
        Lua.pushName name
        Lua.pushHaskellFunction fn
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

-- | Get current timestamp.
luaNow :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaNow = do
    now <- liftIO getCurrentTime
    let seconds = realToFrac $ utcTimeToPOSIXSeconds now

{"filepath": "./src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox/Modules/Text.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}
{-# LANGUAGE ScopedTypeVariables #-}

{- | UTF-8 text utilities module for LuaToolbox.
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Text (
    registerTextModule,
) where

import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified HsLua as Lua

-- | Register the text module in the Lua state.
registerTextModule :: Lua.State -> IO ()
registerTextModule lstate = Lua.runWith lstate $ do
    Lua.newtable

    addFunction "split" luaSplit
    addFunction "find" luaFind
    addFunction "gsub" luaGsub
    addFunction "trim" luaTrim
    addFunction "startswith" luaStartswith
    addFunction "endswith" luaEndswith
    addFunction "lower" luaLower
    addFunction "upper" luaUpper
    addFunction "len" luaLen
    addFunction "sub" luaSub

    Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "text")
  where
    addFunction name fn = do
        Lua.pushName name
        Lua.pushHaskellFunction fn
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

-- | Split a string by a delimiter.
luaSplit :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaSplit = do
    str <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 2)
    delim <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 2
    let parts = Text.splitOn delim str
    pushTextList parts
    pure 1

-- | Find substring position.
luaFind :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaFind = do
    str <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 2)
    pattern <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 2
    case Text.breakOn pattern str of
        (before, "") -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            pure 1
        (before, _) -> do
            let startPos = Text.length before + 1
            let endPos = startPos + Text.length pattern - 1
            Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral startPos)
            Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral endPos)
            pure 2

-- | Global substitution.
luaGsub :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaGsub = do
    str <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 3)
    pattern <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 2)
    replacement <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 3
    let parts = Text.splitOn pattern str
    let count = length parts - 1
    let result = Text.intercalate replacement parts
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 result)
    Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral count)
    pure 2

-- | Trim whitespace from both ends.
luaTrim :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaTrim = do
    str <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 1
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.strip str)
    pure 1

-- | Check if string starts with prefix.
luaStartswith :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaStartswith = do
    str <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 2)
    prefix <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 2
    Lua.pushboolean (prefix `Text.isPrefixOf` str)
    pure 1

-- | Check if string ends with suffix.
luaEndswith :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaEndswith = do
    str <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 2)
    suffix <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 2
    Lua.pushboolean (suffix `Text.isSuffixOf` str)
    pure 1

-- | Convert to lowercase.
luaLower :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaLower = do
    str <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 1
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.toLower str)
    pure 1

-- | Convert to uppercase.
luaUpper :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaUpper = do
    str <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 1
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.toUpper str)
    pure 1

-- | Get string length.
luaLen :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaLen = do
    str <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 1
    Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral $ Text.length str)
    pure 1

-- | Get substring.
luaSub :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaSub = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop
    str <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop nargs)
    mStart <- Lua.tointeger (Lua.nthTop (nargs - 1))
    Lua.pop (fromIntegral nargs)
    case mStart of
        Nothing -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring "Invalid start index"
            pure 2
        Just startIdx -> do
            let len = Text.length str
            let start = fromIntegral startIdx - 1
            let actualStart = if start < 0 then len + start + 1 else start
            if actualStart < 0 || actualStart >= len
                then do
                    Lua.pushstring ""
                    pure 1
                else do
                    let result = Text.drop actualStart str
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 result)
                    pure 1

-- | Helper to get a text argument from the stack.
getTextArg :: Lua.StackIndex -> Lua.LuaE e Text
getTextArg idx = do
    bs <- Lua.tostring' idx
    pure $ Text.decodeUtf8 bs

-- | Push a list of Text values as a Lua table.
pushTextList :: [Text] -> Lua.LuaE e ()
pushTextList items = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(i, txt) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 txt)
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (zip [1..] items)
"}

registerTextModule :: Lua.State -> IO ()
registerTextModule lstate = Lua.runWith lstate $ do
    Lua.newtable

    addFunction "split" luaSplit
    addFunction "find" luaFind
    addFunction "gsub" luaGsub
    addFunction "trim" luaTrim
    addFunction "startswith" luaStartswith
    addFunction "endswith" luaEndswith
    addFunction "lower" luaLower
    addFunction "upper" luaUpper
    addFunction "len" luaLen
    addFunction "sub" luaSub

    Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "text")
  where
    addFunction name fn = do
        Lua.pushName name
        Lua.pushHaskellFunction fn
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

-- | Split a string by a delimiter.
luaSplit :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaSplit = do
    str <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 2)
    delim <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 2
    let parts = Text.splitOn delim str
    pushTextList parts
    pure 1

-- | Find substring position.
luaFind :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaFind = do
    str <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 2)
    pattern <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 2
    case Text.breakOn pattern str of
        (before, "") -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            pure 1
        (before, _) -> do
            let startPos = Text.length before + 1
            let endPos = startPos + Text.length pattern - 1
            Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral startPos)
            Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral endPos)
            pure 2

-- | Global substitution.
luaGsub :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaGsub = do
    str <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 3)
    pattern <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 2)
    replacement <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 3
    let parts = Text.splitOn pattern str
    let count = length parts - 1
    let result = Text.intercalate replacement parts
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 result)
    Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral count)
    pure 2

-- | Trim whitespace from both ends.
luaTrim :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaTrim = do
    str <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 1
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.strip str)
    pure 1

-- | Check if string starts with prefix.
luaStartswith :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaStartswith = do
    str <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 2)
    prefix <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 2
    Lua.pushboolean (prefix `Text.isPrefixOf` str)
    pure 1

-- | Check if string ends with suffix.
luaEndswith :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaEndswith = do
    str <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 2)
    suffix <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 2
    Lua.pushboolean (suffix `Text.isSuffixOf` str)
    pure 1

-- | Convert to lowercase.
luaLower :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaLower = do
    str <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 1
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.toLower str)
    pure 1

-- | Convert to uppercase.
luaUpper :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaUpper = do
    str <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 1
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.toUpper str)
    pure 1

-- | Get string length.
luaLen :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaLen = do
    str <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 1
    Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral $ Text.length str)
    pure 1

-- | Get substring.
luaSub :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaSub = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop
    str <- getTextArg (Lua.nthTop nargs)
    mStart <- Lua.tointeger (Lua.nthTop (nargs - 1))
    Lua.pop (fromIntegral nargs)
    case mStart of
        Nothing -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring "Invalid start index"
            pure 2
        Just startIdx -> do
            let len = Text.length str
            let start = fromIntegral startIdx - 1
            let actualStart = if start < 0 then len + start + 1 else start
            if actualStart < 0 || actualStart >= len
                then do
                    Lua.pushstring ""
                    pure 1
                else do
                    let result = Text.drop actualStart str
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 result)
                    pure 1

-- | Helper to get a text argument from the stack.
getTextArg :: Lua.StackIndex -> Lua.LuaE e Text
getTextArg idx = do
    bs <- Lua.tostring' idx
    pure $ Text.decodeUtf8 bs

-- | Push a list of Text values as a Lua table.
pushTextList :: [Text] -> Lua.LuaE e ()
pushTextList items = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(i, txt) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 txt)
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (zip [1..] items)
"}

function.arguments:
{"filepath": "./src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox/Modules/Json.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}
{-# LANGUAGE ScopedTypeVariables #-}

{- | JSON module for LuaToolbox.
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Json (
    registerJsonModule,
) where

import qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson
import qualified Data.Aeson.Text as AesonText
import Data.Scientific (Scientific, floatingOrInteger)
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Lazy as LazyText
import qualified Data.Vector as Vector
import qualified HsLua as Lua

import qualified Data.Aeson.KeyMap as KeyMap
import qualified Data.Aeson.Key as AesonKey

-- | Register the json module in the Lua state.
registerJsonModule :: Lua.State -> IO ()
registerJsonModule lstate = Lua.runWith lstate $ do
    Lua.newtable

    Lua.pushName "encode"
    Lua.pushHaskellFunction luaEncode
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

    Lua.pushName "decode"
    Lua.pushHaskellFunction luaDecode
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

    Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "json")

-- | Encode a Lua value to JSON string.
luaEncode :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaEncode = do
    jsonVal <- luaToAesonValue
    let txt = LazyText.toStrict $ AesonText.encodeToLazyText jsonVal
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 txt)
    pure 1

-- | Decode a JSON string to a Lua value.
luaDecode :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaDecode = do
    str <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 1
    case Aeson.eitherDecodeStrict' str of
        Left err -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack err)
            pure 2
        Right val -> do
            aesonToLuaValue val
            pure 1

-- | Convert Lua value at top of stack to Aeson Value.
luaToAesonValue :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
luaToAesonValue = do
    ltype <- Lua.ltype (Lua.nthTop 1)
    case ltype of
        Lua.TypeNil -> do
            Lua.pop 1
            pure Aeson.Null
        Lua.TypeBoolean -> do
            b <- Lua.toboolean (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.Bool b
        Lua.TypeNumber -> do
            mNum <- Lua.tonumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            case mNum of
                Nothing -> pure Aeson.Null
                Just n -> pure $ Aeson.Number $ fromFloatDigits n
        Lua.TypeString -> do
            s <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.String (Text.decodeUtf8 s)
        Lua.TypeTable -> do
            convertTable
        _ -> do
            str <- luaValueToString
            pure $ Aeson.String str
  where
    fromFloatDigits :: Double -> Scientific
    fromFloatDigits = read . show

    luaValueToString :: Lua.LuaE e Text
    luaValueToString = do
        Lua.pushglobaltable
        Lua.pushName (Lua.Name "tostring")
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)
        _ <- Lua.pcall 1 1 Nothing
        s <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
        Lua.pop 1
        pure $ Text.decodeUtf8 s

-- | Convert Lua table to Aeson Value.
convertTable :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertTable = do
    isArr <- isArray
    if isArr
        then convertArray
        else convertObject

-- | Check if table at top of stack is an array.
isArray :: Lua.LuaE e Bool
isArray = do
    Lua.pushnil
    isSequential 1
  where
    isSequential :: Int -> Lua.LuaE e Bool
    isSequential expectedIdx = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then pure True
            else do
                isNum <- Lua.isnumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
                if isNum
                    then do
                        mIdx <- Lua.tointeger (Lua.nthTop 1)
                        Lua.pop 2
                        case mIdx of
                            Just idx
                                | idx == fromIntegral expectedIdx ->
                                    isSequential (expectedIdx + 1)
                            _ -> pure False
                    else do
                        Lua.pop 2
                        pure False

-- | Convert array table to JSON Array.
convertArray :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertArray = do
    vals <- collectArrayValues
    pure $ Aeson.Array (Vector.fromList vals)

collectArrayValues :: Lua.LuaE e [Aeson.Value]
collectArrayValues = do
    len' <- Lua.rawlen (Lua.nthTop 1)
    vals <- mapM (\i -> do
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        val <- luaToAesonValue
        pure val
        ) [1 .. fromIntegral len']
    Lua.pop 1
    pure vals

-- | Convert object table to JSON Object.
convertObject :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertObject = do
    pairs <- collectObjectPairs
    pure $ Aeson.Object (KeyMap.fromList pairs)

collectObjectPairs :: Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
collectObjectPairs = do
    Lua.pushnil
    go []
  where
    go :: [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)] -> Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
    go acc = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then do
                Lua.pop 1
                pure acc
            else do
                key <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
                val <- luaToAesonValue
                Lua.pop 1
                go ((AesonKey.fromText (Text.decodeUtf8 key), val) : acc)

-- | Convert Aeson Value to Lua value.
aesonToLuaValue :: Aeson.Value -> Lua.LuaE e ()
aesonToLuaValue Aeson.Null = Lua.pushnil
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Bool b) = Lua.pushboolean b
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Number n) = 
    case floatingOrInteger n of
        Left d -> Lua.pushnumber d
        Right i -> Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.String t) = Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 t)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Array arr) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(i, val) -> do
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral (i + 1))
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (zip [0..] (Vector.toList arr))
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Object obj) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(k, val) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ AesonKey.toText k)
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (KeyMap.toList obj)
"}

-- | Convert Lua table to Aeson Value.
convertTable :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertTable = do
    isArr <- isArray
    if isArr
        then convertArray
        else convertObject

-- | Check if table at top of stack is an array.
isArray :: Lua.LuaE e Bool
isArray = do
    Lua.pushnil
    isSequential 1
  where
    isSequential :: Int -> Lua.LuaE e Bool
    isSequential expectedIdx = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then pure True
            else do
                isNum <- Lua.isnumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
                if isNum
                    then do
                        mIdx <- Lua.tointeger (Lua.nthTop 1)
                        Lua.pop 2
                        case mIdx of
                            Just idx
                                | idx == fromIntegral expectedIdx ->
                                    isSequential (expectedIdx + 1)
                            _ -> pure False
                    else do
                        Lua.pop 2
                        pure False

-- | Convert array table to JSON Array.
convertArray :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertArray = do
    vals <- collectArrayValues
    pure $ Aeson.Array (Vector.fromList vals)

collectArrayValues :: Lua.LuaE e [Aeson.Value]
collectArrayValues = do
    len' <- Lua.rawlen (Lua.nthTop 1)
    vals <- mapM (\i -> do
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        val <- luaToAesonValue
        pure val
        ) [1 .. fromIntegral len']
    Lua.pop 1
    pure vals

-- | Convert object table to JSON Object.
convertObject :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertObject = do
    pairs <- collectObjectPairs
    pure $ Aeson.Object (KeyMap.fromList pairs)

collectObjectPairs :: Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
collectObjectPairs = do
    Lua.pushnil
    go []
  where
    go :: [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)] -> Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
    go acc = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then do
                Lua.pop 1
                pure acc
            else do
                key <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
                val <- luaToAesonValue
                Lua.pop 1
                go ((AesonKey.fromText (Text.decodeUtf8 key), val) : acc)

-- | Convert Aeson Value to Lua value.
aesonToLuaValue :: Aeson.Value -> Lua.LuaE e ()
aesonToLuaValue Aeson.Null = Lua.pushnil
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Bool b) = Lua.pushboolean b
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Number n) = 
    case floatingOrInteger n of
        Left d -> Lua.pushnumber d
        Right i -> Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.String t) = Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 t)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Array arr) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(i, val) -> do
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral (i + 1))
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (zip [0..] (Vector.toList arr))
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Object obj) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(k, val) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ AesonKey.toText k)
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (KeyMap.toList obj)
"}

function.arguments:
{"filepath": "./src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox/Modules/Json.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}
{-# LANGUAGE ScopedTypeVariables #-}

{- | JSON module for LuaToolbox.

Provides JSON encoding and decoding functions to Lua scripts.
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Json (
    registerJsonModule,
) where

import qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson
import qualified Data.Aeson.Text as AesonText
import qualified Data.ByteString as BS
import Data.Scientific (Scientific, floatingOrInteger)
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Lazy as LazyText
import qualified Data.Vector as Vector
import qualified HsLua as Lua

import qualified Data.Aeson.KeyMap as KeyMap
import qualified Data.Aeson.Key as AesonKey

-- | Register the json module in the Lua state.
registerJsonModule :: Lua.State -> IO ()
registerJsonModule lstate = do
    -- Create C function pointers
    encodeFn <- Lua.wrapHaskellFunction luaEncode
    decodeFn <- Lua.wrapHaskellFunction luaDecode
    Lua.runWith lstate $ do
        Lua.newtable

        -- json.encode(value) -> string
        Lua.pushName "encode"
        Lua.pushcfunction encodeFn
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

        -- json.decode(string) -> value
        Lua.pushName "decode"
        Lua.pushcfunction decodeFn
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

        -- Register as 'json' module
        Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "json")

-- | Encode a Lua value to JSON string.
luaEncode :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaEncode = do
    jsonVal <- luaToAesonValue
    let txt = LazyText.toStrict $ AesonText.encodeToLazyText jsonVal
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 txt)
    pure 1

-- | Decode a JSON string to a Lua value.
luaDecode :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaDecode = do
    str <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 1
    case Aeson.eitherDecodeStrict' str of
        Left err -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack err)
            pure 2
        Right val -> do
            aesonToLuaValue val
            pure 1

-- | Convert Lua value at top of stack to Aeson Value.
luaToAesonValue :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
luaToAesonValue = do
    ltype <- Lua.ltype (Lua.nthTop 1)
    case ltype of
        Lua.TypeNil -> do
            Lua.pop 1
            pure Aeson.Null
        Lua.TypeBoolean -> do
            b <- Lua.toboolean (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.Bool b
        Lua.TypeNumber -> do
            mNum <- Lua.tonumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            case mNum of
                Nothing -> pure Aeson.Null
                Just n -> pure $ Aeson.Number $ fromFloatDigits n
        Lua.TypeString -> do
            s <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.String (Text.decodeUtf8 s)
        Lua.TypeTable -> do
            convertTable
        _ -> do
            str <- luaValueToString
            pure $ Aeson.String str
  where
    fromFloatDigits :: Double -> Scientific
    fromFloatDigits = read . show

    luaValueToString :: Lua.LuaE e Text
    luaValueToString = do
        Lua.pushglobaltable
        Lua.pushName (Lua.Name "tostring")
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)
        _ <- Lua.pcall 1 1 Nothing
        s <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
        Lua.pop 1
        pure $ Text.decodeUtf8 s

-- | Convert Lua table to Aeson Value (object or array).
convertTable :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertTable = do
    isArr <- isArray
    if isArr
        then convertArray
        else convertObject

-- | Check if table at top of stack is an array.
isArray :: Lua.LuaE e Bool
isArray = do
    Lua.pushnil
    isSequential 1
  where
    isSequential :: Int -> Lua.LuaE e Bool
    isSequential expectedIdx = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then pure True
            else do
                isNum <- Lua.isnumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
                if isNum
                    then do
                        mIdx <- Lua.tointeger (Lua.nthTop 1)
                        Lua.pop 2
                        case mIdx of
                            Just idx
                                | idx == fromIntegral expectedIdx ->
                                    isSequential (expectedIdx + 1)
                            _ -> pure False
                    else do
                        Lua.pop 2
                        pure False

-- | Convert array table to JSON Array.
convertArray :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertArray = do
    vals <- collectArrayValues
    pure $ Aeson.Array (Vector.fromList vals)

-- | Collect values from array table.
collectArrayValues :: Lua.LuaE e [Aeson.Value]
collectArrayValues = do
    len' <- Lua.rawlen (Lua.nthTop 1)
    vals <- mapM (\i -> do
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        val <- luaToAesonValue
        pure val
        ) [1 .. fromIntegral len']
    Lua.pop 1  -- pop the table
    pure vals

-- | Convert object table to JSON Object.
convertObject :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertObject = do
    pairs <- collectObjectPairs
    pure $ Aeson.Object (KeyMap.fromList pairs)

-- | Collect key-value pairs from object table.
collectObjectPairs :: Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
collectObjectPairs = do
    Lua.pushnil
    go []
  where
    go :: [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)] -> Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
    go acc = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then do
                Lua.pop 1
                pure acc
            else do
                key <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
                val <- luaToAesonValue
                Lua.pop 1
                go ((AesonKey.fromText (Text.decodeUtf8 key), val) : acc)

-- | Convert Aeson Value to Lua value and push onto stack.
aesonToLuaValue :: Aeson.Value -> Lua.LuaE e ()
aesonToLuaValue Aeson.Null = Lua.pushnil
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Bool b) = Lua.pushboolean b
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Number n) = 
    case floatingOrInteger n of
        Left d -> Lua.pushnumber d
        Right i -> Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.String t) = Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 t)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Array arr) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(i, val) -> do
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral (i + 1))
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (zip [0..] (Vector.toList arr))
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Object obj) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(k, val) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ AesonKey.toText k)
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (KeyMap.toList obj)
"}

message.content: Let me create a helper for the FFI wrapper and fix all modules:
message.reasoning_content:
The issue is that I need to use the FFI properly to create C-callable functions. The `Lua.CFunction` is a `FunPtr PreCFunction`, which means I need to create a foreign wrapper using `foreign import ccall "wrapper"`.

Let me create a helper module that provides the proper FFI wrappers for pushing Haskell functions as Lua C functions, and then simplify all the modules to use this approach.

function.arguments:
{"filepath": "./src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox/Modules/Json.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}
{-# LANGUAGE ScopedTypeVariables #-}

{- | JSON module for LuaToolbox.

Provides JSON encoding and decoding functions to Lua scripts.
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Json (
    registerJsonModule,
) where

import qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson
import qualified Data.Aeson.Text as AesonText
import qualified Data.ByteString as BS
import Data.Scientific (Scientific, floatingOrInteger)
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Lazy as LazyText
import qualified Data.Vector as Vector
import qualified HsLua as Lua

import qualified Data.Aeson.KeyMap as KeyMap
import qualified Data.Aeson.Key as AesonKey

-- | Register the json module in the Lua state.
registerJsonModule :: Lua.State -> IO ()
registerJsonModule lstate = do
    -- Create C function pointers
    encodeFn <- Lua.wrapHaskellFunction luaEncode
    decodeFn <- Lua.wrapHaskellFunction luaDecode
    Lua.runWith lstate $ do
        Lua.newtable

        -- json.encode(value) -> string
        Lua.pushName "encode"
        Lua.pushcfunction encodeFn
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

        -- json.decode(string) -> value
        Lua.pushName "decode"
        Lua.pushcfunction decodeFn
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

        -- Register as 'json' module
        Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "json")

-- | Encode a Lua value to JSON string.
luaEncode :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaEncode = do
    jsonVal <- luaToAesonValue
    let txt = LazyText.toStrict $ AesonText.encodeToLazyText jsonVal
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 txt)
    pure 1

-- | Decode a JSON string to a Lua value.
luaDecode :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaDecode = do
    str <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 1
    case Aeson.eitherDecodeStrict' str of
        Left err -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack err)
            pure 2
        Right val -> do
            aesonToLuaValue val
            pure 1

-- | Convert Lua value at top of stack to Aeson Value.
luaToAesonValue :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
luaToAesonValue = do
    ltype <- Lua.ltype (Lua.nthTop 1)
    case ltype of
        Lua.TypeNil -> do
            Lua.pop 1
            pure Aeson.Null
        Lua.TypeBoolean -> do
            b <- Lua.toboolean (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.Bool b
        Lua.TypeNumber -> do
            mNum <- Lua.tonumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            case mNum of
                Nothing -> pure Aeson.Null
                Just n -> pure $ Aeson.Number $ fromFloatDigits n
        Lua.TypeString -> do
            s <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.String (Text.decodeUtf8 s)
        Lua.TypeTable -> do
            convertTable
        _ -> do
            str <- luaValueToString
            pure $ Aeson.String str
  where
    fromFloatDigits :: Double -> Scientific
    fromFloatDigits = read . show

    luaValueToString :: Lua.LuaE e Text
    luaValueToString = do
        Lua.pushglobaltable
        Lua.pushName (Lua.Name "tostring")
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)
        _ <- Lua.pcall 1 1 Nothing
        s <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
        Lua.pop 1
        pure $ Text.decodeUtf8 s

-- | Convert Lua table to Aeson Value (object or array).
convertTable :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertTable = do
    isArr <- isArray
    if isArr
        then convertArray
        else convertObject

-- | Check if table at top of stack is an array.
isArray :: Lua.LuaE e Bool
isArray = do
    Lua.pushnil
    isSequential 1
  where
    isSequential :: Int -> Lua.LuaE e Bool
    isSequential expectedIdx = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then pure True
            else do
                isNum <- Lua.isnumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
                if isNum
                    then do
                        mIdx <- Lua.tointeger (Lua.nthTop 1)
                        Lua.pop 2
                        case mIdx of
                            Just idx
                                | idx == fromIntegral expectedIdx ->
                                    isSequential (expectedIdx + 1)
                            _ -> pure False
                    else do
                        Lua.pop 2
                        pure False

-- | Convert array table to JSON Array.
convertArray :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertArray = do
    vals <- collectArrayValues
    pure $ Aeson.Array (Vector.fromList vals)

-- | Collect values from array table.
collectArrayValues :: Lua.LuaE e [Aeson.Value]
collectArrayValues = do
    len' <- Lua.rawlen (Lua.nthTop 1)
    vals <- mapM (\i -> do
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        val <- luaToAesonValue
        pure val
        ) [1 .. fromIntegral len']
    Lua.pop 1  -- pop the table
    pure vals

-- | Convert object table to JSON Object.
convertObject :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertObject = do
    pairs <- collectObjectPairs
    pure $ Aeson.Object (KeyMap.fromList pairs)

-- | Collect key-value pairs from object table.
collectObjectPairs :: Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
collectObjectPairs = do
    Lua.pushnil
    go []
  where
    go :: [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)] -> Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
    go acc = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then do
                Lua.pop 1
                pure acc
            else do
                key <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
                val <- luaToAesonValue
                Lua.pop 1
                go ((AesonKey.fromText (Text.decodeUtf8 key), val) : acc)

-- | Convert Aeson Value to Lua value and push onto stack.
aesonToLuaValue :: Aeson.Value -> Lua.LuaE e ()
aesonToLuaValue Aeson.Null = Lua.pushnil
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Bool b) = Lua.pushboolean b
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Number n) = 
    case floatingOrInteger n of
        Left d -> Lua.pushnumber d
        Right i -> Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.String t) = Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 t)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Array arr) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(i, val) -> do
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral (i + 1))
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (zip [0..] (Vector.toList arr))
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Object obj) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(k, val) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ AesonKey.toText k)
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (KeyMap.toList obj)
"}

function.name: bash_write-file

contents.userPrompt:
You are an expert Haskell and Bash programmer specialized in writing clean, idiomatic, and efficient Haskell code.
When writing Bash code:
- Avoid bash-specific logic but bash-ism are fine.
- Document required shell tools at the top of the bash script.
When writing Haskell code:
- Use modern Haskell practices (base >= 4.14)
- Prefer pure functions and explicit type signatures
- Use standard libraries when appropriate
- Add clear comments for complex logic
- Follow consistent formatting
Use list-files to explore the project structure, read-file to understand existing code, write-file to create or modify files, and check-code to verify your work.
Always compile code before considering the job done.
Your output format may be stored as commit message: hence the first line of your last message should be a summary line.

function.arguments: {"action": "compile"}

You are an expert Haskell and Bash programmer specialized in writing clean, idiomatic, and efficient Haskell code.
When writing Bash code:
- Avoid bash-specific logic but bash-ism are fine.
- Document required shell tools at the top of the bash script.
When writing Haskell code:
- Use modern Haskell practices (base >= 4.14)
- Prefer pure functions and explicit type signatures

- Add clear comments for complex logic
- Follow consistent formatting
Use list-files to explore the project structure, read-file to understand existing code, write-file to create or modify files, and check-code to verify your work.

{"filepath": "./src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox/Modules/Json.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}
{-# LANGUAGE ScopedTypeVariables #-}
{-# LANGUAGE ForeignFunctionInterface #-}

{- | JSON module for LuaToolbox.

Provides JSON encoding and decoding functions to Lua scripts.
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Json (
    registerJsonModule,
) where

import qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson
import qualified Data.Aeson.Text as AesonText
import qualified Data.ByteString as BS
import Data.Scientific (Scientific, floatingOrInteger)
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Lazy as LazyText
import qualified Data.Vector as Vector
import qualified HsLua as Lua
import Foreign.Ptr (FunPtr)

import qualified Data.Aeson.KeyMap as KeyMap
import qualified Data.Aeson.Key as AesonKey

-- | Foreign export wrappers for Lua C functions
foreign export ccall "json_lua_encode" luaEncodeC :: Lua.CFunction
foreign export ccall "json_lua_decode" luaDecodeC :: Lua.CFunction

-- | C-callable wrapper for luaEncode
luaEncodeC :: Lua.CFunction
luaEncodeC = Lua.toCFunction luaEncode

-- | C-callable wrapper for luaDecode  
luaDecodeC :: Lua.CFunction
luaDecodeC = Lua.toCFunction luaDecode

-- | Register the json module in the Lua state.
registerJsonModule :: Lua.State -> IO ()
registerJsonModule lstate = Lua.runWith lstate $ do
    Lua.newtable  -- Create module table

    -- json.encode(value) -> string
    Lua.pushName "encode"
    Lua.pushcfunction luaEncodeC
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

    -- json.decode(string) -> value
    Lua.pushName "decode"
    Lua.pushcfunction luaDecodeC
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

    -- Register as 'json' module
    Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "json")

-- | Encode a Lua value to JSON string.
luaEncode :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaEncode = do
    jsonVal <- luaToAesonValue
    let txt = LazyText.toStrict $ AesonText.encodeToLazyText jsonVal
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 txt)
    pure 1

-- | Decode a JSON string to a Lua value.
luaDecode :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaDecode = do
    str <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 1
    case Aeson.eitherDecodeStrict' str of
        Left err -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack err)
            pure 2
        Right val -> do
            aesonToLuaValue val
            pure 1

-- | Convert Lua value at top of stack to Aeson Value.
luaToAesonValue :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
luaToAesonValue = do
    ltype <- Lua.ltype (Lua.nthTop 1)
    case ltype of
        Lua.TypeNil -> do
            Lua.pop 1
            pure Aeson.Null
        Lua.TypeBoolean -> do
            b <- Lua.toboolean (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.Bool b
        Lua.TypeNumber -> do
            mNum <- Lua.tonumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            case mNum of
                Nothing -> pure Aeson.Null
                Just n -> pure $ Aeson.Number $ fromFloatDigits n
        Lua.TypeString -> do
            s <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.String (Text.decodeUtf8 s)
        Lua.TypeTable -> do
            convertTable
        _ -> do
            str <- luaValueToString
            pure $ Aeson.String str
  where
    fromFloatDigits :: Double -> Scientific
    fromFloatDigits = read . show

    luaValueToString :: Lua.LuaE e Text
    luaValueToString = do
        Lua.pushglobaltable
        Lua.pushName (Lua.Name "tostring")
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)
        _ <- Lua.pcall 1 1 Nothing
        s <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
        Lua.pop 1
        pure $ Text.decodeUtf8 s

-- | Convert Lua table to Aeson Value (object or array).
convertTable :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertTable = do
    isArr <- isArray
    if isArr
        then convertArray
        else convertObject

-- | Check if table at top of stack is an array.
isArray :: Lua.LuaE e Bool
isArray = do
    Lua.pushnil
    isSequential 1
  where
    isSequential :: Int -> Lua.LuaE e Bool
    isSequential expectedIdx = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then pure True
            else do
                isNum <- Lua.isnumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
                if isNum
                    then do
                        mIdx <- Lua.tointeger (Lua.nthTop 1)
                        Lua.pop 2
                        case mIdx of
                            Just idx
                                | idx == fromIntegral expectedIdx ->
                                    isSequential (expectedIdx + 1)
                            _ -> pure False
                    else do
                        Lua.pop 2
                        pure False

-- | Convert array table to JSON Array.
convertArray :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertArray = do
    vals <- collectArrayValues
    pure $ Aeson.Array (Vector.fromList vals)

-- | Collect values from array table.
collectArrayValues :: Lua.LuaE e [Aeson.Value]
collectArrayValues = do
    len' <- Lua.rawlen (Lua.nthTop 1)
    vals <- mapM (\i -> do
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        val <- luaToAesonValue
        pure val
        ) [1 .. fromIntegral len']
    Lua.pop 1  -- pop the table
    pure vals

-- | Convert object table to JSON Object.
convertObject :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertObject = do
    pairs <- collectObjectPairs
    pure $ Aeson.Object (KeyMap.fromList pairs)

-- | Collect key-value pairs from object table.
collectObjectPairs :: Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
collectObjectPairs = do
    Lua.pushnil
    go []
  where
    go :: [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)] -> Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
    go acc = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then do
                Lua.pop 1
                pure acc
            else do
                key <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
                val <- luaToAesonValue
                Lua.pop 1
                go ((AesonKey.fromText (Text.decodeUtf8 key), val) : acc)

-- | Convert Aeson Value to Lua value and push onto stack.
aesonToLuaValue :: Aeson.Value -> Lua.LuaE e ()
aesonToLuaValue Aeson.Null = Lua.pushnil
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Bool b) = Lua.pushboolean b
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Number n) = 
    case floatingOrInteger n of
        Left d -> Lua.pushnumber d
        Right i -> Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.String t) = Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 t)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Array arr) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(i, val) -> do
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral (i + 1))
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (zip [0..] (Vector.toList arr))
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Object obj) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(k, val) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ AesonKey.toText k)
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (KeyMap.toList obj)
"}

luaToAesonValue :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
luaToAesonValue = do
    ltype <- Lua.ltype (Lua.nthTop 1)
    case ltype of
        Lua.TypeNil -> do
            Lua.pop 1
            pure Aeson.Null
        Lua.TypeBoolean -> do
            b <- Lua.toboolean (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.Bool b
        Lua.TypeNumber -> do
            mNum <- Lua.tonumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            case mNum of
                Nothing -> pure Aeson.Null
                Just n -> pure $ Aeson.Number $ fromFloatDigits n
        Lua.TypeString -> do
            s <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.String (Text.decodeUtf8 s)
        Lua.TypeTable -> do
            convertTable
        _ -> do
            str <- luaValueToString
            pure $ Aeson.String str
  where
    fromFloatDigits :: Double -> Scientific
    fromFloatDigits = read . show

    luaValueToString :: Lua.LuaE e Text
    luaValueToString = do
        Lua.pushglobaltable
        Lua.pushName (Lua.Name "tostring")
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)
        _ <- Lua.pcall 1 1 Nothing
        s <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
        Lua.pop 1
        pure $ Text.decodeUtf8 s

-- | Convert Lua table to Aeson Value (object or array).
convertTable :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertTable = do
    isArr <- isArray
    if isArr
        then convertArray
        else convertObject

-- | Check if table at top of stack is an array.
isArray :: Lua.LuaE e Bool
isArray = do
    Lua.pushnil
    isSequential 1
  where
    isSequential :: Int -> Lua.LuaE e Bool
    isSequential expectedIdx = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then pure True
            else do
                isNum <- Lua.isnumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
                if isNum
                    then do
                        mIdx <- Lua.tointeger (Lua.nthTop 1)
                        Lua.pop 2
                        case mIdx of
                            Just idx
                                | idx == fromIntegral expectedIdx ->
                                    isSequential (expectedIdx + 1)
                            _ -> pure False
                    else do
                        Lua.pop 2
                        pure False

-- | Convert array table to JSON Array.
convertArray :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertArray = do
    vals <- collectArrayValues
    pure $ Aeson.Array (Vector.fromList vals)

-- | Collect values from array table.
collectArrayValues :: Lua.LuaE e [Aeson.Value]
collectArrayValues = do
    len' <- Lua.rawlen (Lua.nthTop 1)
    vals <- mapM (\i -> do
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        val <- luaToAesonValue
        pure val
        ) [1 .. fromIntegral len']
    Lua.pop 1  -- pop the table
    pure vals

-- | Convert object table to JSON Object.
convertObject :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertObject = do
    pairs <- collectObjectPairs
    pure $ Aeson.Object (KeyMap.fromList pairs)

-- | Collect key-value pairs from object table.
collectObjectPairs :: Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
collectObjectPairs = do
    Lua.pushnil
    go []
  where
    go :: [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)] -> Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
    go acc = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then do
                Lua.pop 1
                pure acc
            else do
                key <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
                val <- luaToAesonValue
                Lua.pop 1
                go ((AesonKey.fromText (Text.decodeUtf8 key), val) : acc)

-- | Convert Aeson Value to Lua value and push onto stack.
aesonToLuaValue :: Aeson.Value -> Lua.LuaE e ()
aesonToLuaValue Aeson.Null = Lua.pushnil
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Bool b) = Lua.pushboolean b
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Number n) = 
    case floatingOrInteger n of
        Left d -> Lua.pushnumber d
        Right i -> Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.String t) = Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 t)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Array arr) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(i, val) -> do
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral (i + 1))
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (zip [0..] (Vector.toList arr))
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Object obj) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(k, val) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ AesonKey.toText k)
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (KeyMap.toList obj)
"}
