{"filepath": "./src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox/Modules/Json.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}
{-# LANGUAGE ScopedTypeVariables #-}

{- | JSON module for LuaToolbox.

Provides JSON encoding and decoding functions to Lua scripts:

> local json = require("json")
> local encoded = json.encode({foo = "bar", count = 42})
> -- encoded: '{"foo":"bar","count":42}'
> local decoded = json.decode('{"foo":"bar"}')
> -- decoded: {foo = "bar"}

Implementation notes:
* JSON numbers become Lua numbers (doubles). Large integers may lose precision.
* JSON null becomes Lua nil.
* JSON arrays become Lua tables with integer keys starting at 1.
* JSON objects become Lua tables with string keys.
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Json (
    registerJsonModule,
) where

import qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson
import qualified Data.Aeson.Text as AesonText
import qualified Data.ByteString as BS
import Data.Scientific (Scientific, floatingOrInteger)
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Lazy as LazyText
import qualified Data.Vector as Vector
import qualified HsLua as Lua

import qualified Data.Aeson.KeyMap as KeyMap
import qualified Data.Aeson.Key as AesonKey

-- | Register the json module in the Lua state.
registerJsonModule :: Lua.State -> IO ()
registerJsonModule lstate = Lua.runWith lstate $ do
    Lua.newtable  -- Create module table

    -- json.encode(value) -> string
    Lua.pushName "encode"
    Lua.push HaskellFn luaEncode
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

    -- json.decode(string) -> value
    Lua.pushName "decode"
    Lua.push HaskellFn luaDecode
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

    -- Register as 'json' module
    Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "json")

-- | Wrapper type for Haskell functions that can be pushed to Lua
data HaskellFn = forall e. HaskellFn (Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults)

instance Lua.Pushable HaskellFn where
    push (HaskellFn fn) = Lua.pushHaskellFunction fn

-- | Encode a Lua value to JSON string.
-- Lua: json.encode(value) -> string
luaEncode :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaEncode = do
    -- Convert Lua value to Aeson Value, then to string
    jsonVal <- luaToAesonValue
    let txt = LazyText.toStrict $ AesonText.encodeToLazyText jsonVal
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 txt)
    pure 1

-- | Decode a JSON string to a Lua value.
-- Lua: json.decode(string) -> value or nil, error
luaDecode :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaDecode = do
    -- Get string argument
    str <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 1
    case Aeson.eitherDecodeStrict' str of
        Left err -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack err)
            pure 2  -- nil, error
        Right val -> do
            aesonToLuaValue val
            pure 1

-- | Convert Lua value at top of stack to Aeson Value.
luaToAesonValue :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
luaToAesonValue = do
    ltype <- Lua.ltype (Lua.nthTop 1)
    case ltype of
        Lua.TypeNil -> do
            Lua.pop 1
            pure Aeson.Null
        Lua.TypeBoolean -> do
            b <- Lua.toboolean (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.Bool b
        Lua.TypeNumber -> do
            mNum <- Lua.tonumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            case mNum of
                Nothing -> pure Aeson.Null
                Just n -> pure $ Aeson.Number $ fromFloatDigits n
        Lua.TypeString -> do
            s <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.String (Text.decodeUtf8 s)
        Lua.TypeTable -> do
            convertTable
        _ -> do
            -- For other types, convert to string
            str <- luaValueToString
            pure $ Aeson.String str
  where
    fromFloatDigits :: Double -> Scientific
    fromFloatDigits = read . show

    luaValueToString :: Lua.LuaE e Text
    luaValueToString = do
        Lua.pushglobaltable
        Lua.pushName (Lua.Name "tostring")
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)
        _ <- Lua.pcall 1 1 Nothing
        s <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
        Lua.pop 1
        pure $ Text.decodeUtf8 s

-- | Convert Lua table to Aeson Value (object or array).
convertTable :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertTable = do
    isArr <- isArray
    if isArr
        then convertArray
        else convertObject

-- | Check if table at top of stack is an array.
-- An array has sequential integer keys starting at 1.
isArray :: Lua.LuaE e Bool
isArray = do
    Lua.pushnil
    isSequential 1
  where
    isSequential :: Int -> Lua.LuaE e Bool
    isSequential expectedIdx = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then pure True
            else do
                isNum <- Lua.isnumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
                if isNum
                    then do
                        mIdx <- Lua.tointeger (Lua.nthTop 1)
                        Lua.pop 2
                        case mIdx of
                            Just idx
                                | idx == fromIntegral expectedIdx ->
                                    isSequential (expectedIdx + 1)
                            _ -> pure False
                    else do
                        Lua.pop 2
                        pure False

-- | Convert array table to JSON Array.
convertArray :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertArray = do
    vals <- collectArrayValues
    pure $ Aeson.Array (Vector.fromList vals)

-- | Collect values from array table.
collectArrayValues :: Lua.LuaE e [Aeson.Value]
collectArrayValues = do
    len' <- Lua.rawlen (Lua.nthTop 1)
    vals <- mapM (\i -> do
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        val <- luaToAesonValue
        pure val
        ) [1 .. fromIntegral len']
    Lua.pop 1  -- pop the table
    pure vals

-- | Convert object table to JSON Object.
convertObject :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertObject = do
    pairs <- collectObjectPairs
    pure $ Aeson.Object (KeyMap.fromList pairs)

-- | Collect key-value pairs from object table.
collectObjectPairs :: Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
collectObjectPairs = do
    Lua.pushnil
    go []
  where
    go :: [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)] -> Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
    go acc = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then do
                Lua.pop 1  -- pop the table
                pure acc
            else do
                key <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
                val <- luaToJsonValue
                Lua.pop 1  -- pop key
                go ((AesonKey.fromText (Text.decodeUtf8 key), val) : acc)

-- | Convert Aeson Value to Lua value and push onto stack.
aesonToLuaValue :: Aeson.Value -> Lua.LuaE e ()
aesonToLuaValue Aeson.Null = Lua.pushnil
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Bool b) = Lua.pushboolean b
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Number n) = 
    case floatingOrInteger n of
        Left d -> Lua.pushnumber d
        Right i -> Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.String t) = Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 t)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Array arr) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(i, val) -> do
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral (i + 1))  -- key (1-indexed)
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)  -- move key below value
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (zip [0..] (Vector.toList arr))
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Object obj) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(k, val) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ AesonKey.toText k)
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (KeyMap.toList obj)
"}

message.content: Let me look at the HsLua package documentation. The key issue is that we need to use `Lua.push` with a proper marshaller. Let me rewrite the modules with a simpler approach using a wrapper:
message.reasoning_content:
I see. The HsLua API is quite different. Let me look at the HsLua package to understand the correct way to push Haskell functions. Based on the error, `Lua.pushcfunction` expects a `CFunction` (a `FunPtr PreCFunction`), not a `LuaE e NumResults`.

Looking at the errors:
1. `Lua.seti` doesn't exist - need to use `rawseti` or set via the table API
2. `Lua.istype` doesn't exist - need to use `ltype` and compare
3. `Lua.pushcfunction` expects a C function pointer, not a Haskell function
4. `Lua.pushnumber` expects `Lua.Number` (which is `Double`), so that's fine
5. `Lua.pop` expects `Int`, not `StackIndex`

I need to use a different approach for pushing Haskell functions. Looking at HsLua, the proper way is to use `Lua.push` with a `Peekable`/`Poppable` instance or use the marshalling module.

Let me try to look at what functions are available in the hslua-marshalling package that might help. Actually, looking at the version of hslua (>= 2.3), I need to find the correct way to register Haskell functions.

Let me look at the main LuaToolbox module to see how it currently does things, and also search for how hslua expects functions to be pushed.

convertArray :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertArray = do
    vals <- collectArrayValues
    pure $ Aeson.Array (Vector.fromList vals)

-- | Collect values from array table.
collectArrayValues :: Lua.LuaE e [Aeson.Value]
collectArrayValues = do
    len' <- Lua.rawlen (Lua.nthTop 1)
    vals <- mapM (\i -> do
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        val <- luaToAesonValue
        pure val
        ) [1 .. fromIntegral len']
    Lua.pop 1  -- pop the table
    pure vals

-- | Convert object table to JSON Object.
convertObject :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertObject = do
    pairs <- collectObjectPairs
    pure $ Aeson.Object (KeyMap.fromList pairs)

-- | Collect key-value pairs from object table.
collectObjectPairs :: Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
collectObjectPairs = do
    Lua.pushnil
    go []
  where
    go :: [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)] -> Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
    go acc = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then do
                Lua.pop 1  -- pop the table
                pure acc
            else do
                key <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
                val <- luaToJsonValue
                Lua.pop 1  -- pop key
                go ((AesonKey.fromText (Text.decodeUtf8 key), val) : acc)

-- | Convert Aeson Value to Lua value and push onto stack.
aesonToLuaValue :: Aeson.Value -> Lua.LuaE e ()
aesonToLuaValue Aeson.Null = Lua.pushnil
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Bool b) = Lua.pushboolean b
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Number n) = 
    case floatingOrInteger n of
        Left d -> Lua.pushnumber d
        Right i -> Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.String t) = Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 t)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Array arr) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(i, val) -> do
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral (i + 1))  -- key (1-indexed)
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)  -- move key below value
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (zip [0..] (Vector.toList arr))
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Object obj) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(k, val) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ AesonKey.toText k)
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (KeyMap.toList obj)
"}

I see. The HsLua API is quite different. Let me look at the HsLua package to understand the correct way to push Haskell functions. Based on the error, `Lua.pushcfunction` expects a `CFunction` (a `FunPtr PreCFunction`), not a `LuaE e NumResults`.

Looking at the errors:
1. `Lua.seti` doesn't exist - need to use `rawseti` or set via the table API
2. `Lua.istype` doesn't exist - need to use `ltype` and compare
3. `Lua.pushcfunction` expects a C function pointer, not a Haskell function
4. `Lua.pushnumber` expects `Lua.Number` (which is `Double`), so that's fine
5. `Lua.pop` expects `Int`, not `StackIndex`

I need to use a different approach for pushing Haskell functions. Looking at HsLua, the proper way is to use `Lua.push` with a `Peekable`/`Poppable` instance or use the marshalling module.

Let me try to look at what functions are available in the hslua-marshalling package that might help. Actually, looking at the version of hslua (>= 2.3), I need to find the correct way to register Haskell functions.

{"filepath": "./src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox/Modules/Json.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}
{-# LANGUAGE ScopedTypeVariables #-}

{- | JSON module for LuaToolbox.

Provides JSON encoding and decoding functions to Lua scripts:

> local json = require("json")
> local encoded = json.encode({foo = "bar", count = 42})
> -- encoded: '{"foo":"bar","count":42}'
> local decoded = json.decode('{"foo":"bar"}')
> -- decoded: {foo = "bar"}

Implementation notes:
* JSON numbers become Lua numbers (doubles). Large integers may lose precision.
* JSON null becomes Lua nil.
* JSON arrays become Lua tables with integer keys starting at 1.
* JSON objects become Lua tables with string keys.
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Json (
    registerJsonModule,
) where

import qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson
import qualified Data.Aeson.Text as AesonText
import qualified Data.ByteString as BS
import Data.Scientific (Scientific, floatingOrInteger)
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Lazy as LazyText
import qualified Data.Vector as Vector
import qualified HsLua as Lua

import qualified Data.Aeson.KeyMap as KeyMap
import qualified Data.Aeson.Key as AesonKey

-- | Register the json module in the Lua state.
registerJsonModule :: Lua.State -> IO ()
registerJsonModule lstate = Lua.runWith lstate $ do
    Lua.newtable  -- Create module table

    -- json.encode(value) -> string
    Lua.pushName "encode"
    Lua.push HaskellFn luaEncode
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

    -- json.decode(string) -> value
    Lua.pushName "decode"
    Lua.push HaskellFn luaDecode
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

    -- Register as 'json' module
    Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "json")

-- | Wrapper type for Haskell functions that can be pushed to Lua
data HaskellFn = forall e. HaskellFn (Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults)

instance Lua.Pushable HaskellFn where
    push (HaskellFn fn) = Lua.pushHaskellFunction fn

-- | Encode a Lua value to JSON string.
-- Lua: json.encode(value) -> string
luaEncode :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaEncode = do
    -- Convert Lua value to Aeson Value, then to string
    jsonVal <- luaToAesonValue
    let txt = LazyText.toStrict $ AesonText.encodeToLazyText jsonVal
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 txt)
    pure 1

-- | Decode a JSON string to a Lua value.
-- Lua: json.decode(string) -> value or nil, error
luaDecode :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaDecode = do
    -- Get string argument
    str <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 1
    case Aeson.eitherDecodeStrict' str of
        Left err -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack err)
            pure 2  -- nil, error
        Right val -> do
            aesonToLuaValue val
            pure 1

-- | Convert Lua value at top of stack to Aeson Value.
luaToAesonValue :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
luaToAesonValue = do
    ltype <- Lua.ltype (Lua.nthTop 1)
    case ltype of
        Lua.TypeNil -> do
            Lua.pop 1
            pure Aeson.Null
        Lua.TypeBoolean -> do
            b <- Lua.toboolean (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.Bool b
        Lua.TypeNumber -> do
            mNum <- Lua.tonumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            case mNum of
                Nothing -> pure Aeson.Null
                Just n -> pure $ Aeson.Number $ fromFloatDigits n
        Lua.TypeString -> do
            s <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.String (Text.decodeUtf8 s)
        Lua.TypeTable -> do
            convertTable
        _ -> do
            -- For other types, convert to string
            str <- luaValueToString
            pure $ Aeson.String str
  where
    fromFloatDigits :: Double -> Scientific
    fromFloatDigits = read . show

    luaValueToString :: Lua.LuaE e Text
    luaValueToString = do
        Lua.pushglobaltable
        Lua.pushName (Lua.Name "tostring")
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)
        _ <- Lua.pcall 1 1 Nothing
        s <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
        Lua.pop 1
        pure $ Text.decodeUtf8 s

-- | Convert Lua table to Aeson Value (object or array).
convertTable :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertTable = do
    isArr <- isArray
    if isArr
        then convertArray
        else convertObject

-- | Check if table at top of stack is an array.
-- An array has sequential integer keys starting at 1.
isArray :: Lua.LuaE e Bool
isArray = do
    Lua.pushnil
    isSequential 1
  where
    isSequential :: Int -> Lua.LuaE e Bool
    isSequential expectedIdx = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then pure True
            else do
                isNum <- Lua.isnumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
                if isNum
                    then do
                        mIdx <- Lua.tointeger (Lua.nthTop 1)
                        Lua.pop 2
                        case mIdx of
                            Just idx
                                | idx == fromIntegral expectedIdx ->
                                    isSequential (expectedIdx + 1)
                            _ -> pure False
                    else do
                        Lua.pop 2
                        pure False

-- | Convert array table to JSON Array.
convertArray :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertArray = do
    vals <- collectArrayValues
    pure $ Aeson.Array (Vector.fromList vals)

-- | Collect values from array table.
collectArrayValues :: Lua.LuaE e [Aeson.Value]
collectArrayValues = do
    len' <- Lua.rawlen (Lua.nthTop 1)
    vals <- mapM (\i -> do
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        val <- luaToAesonValue
        pure val
        ) [1 .. fromIntegral len']
    Lua.pop 1  -- pop the table
    pure vals

-- | Convert object table to JSON Object.
convertObject :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertObject = do
    pairs <- collectObjectPairs
    pure $ Aeson.Object (KeyMap.fromList pairs)

-- | Collect key-value pairs from object table.
collectObjectPairs :: Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
collectObjectPairs = do
    Lua.pushnil
    go []
  where
    go :: [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)] -> Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
    go acc = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then do
                Lua.pop 1  -- pop the table
                pure acc
            else do
                key <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
                val <- luaToJsonValue
                Lua.pop 1  -- pop key
                go ((AesonKey.fromText (Text.decodeUtf8 key), val) : acc)

-- | Convert Aeson Value to Lua value and push onto stack.
aesonToLuaValue :: Aeson.Value -> Lua.LuaE e ()
aesonToLuaValue Aeson.Null = Lua.pushnil
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Bool b) = Lua.pushboolean b
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Number n) = 
    case floatingOrInteger n of
        Left d -> Lua.pushnumber d
        Right i -> Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.String t) = Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 t)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Array arr) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(i, val) -> do
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral (i + 1))  -- key (1-indexed)
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)  -- move key below value
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (zip [0..] (Vector.toList arr))
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Object obj) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(k, val) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ AesonKey.toText k)
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (KeyMap.toList obj)
"}

function.arguments:
{"filepath": "./src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox/Modules/Json.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}
{-# LANGUAGE ScopedTypeVariables #-}

{- | JSON module for LuaToolbox.

Provides JSON encoding and decoding functions to Lua scripts:

> local json = require("json")
> local encoded = json.encode({foo = "bar", count = 42})
> -- encoded: '{"foo":"bar","count":42}'
> local decoded = json.decode('{"foo":"bar"}')
> -- decoded: {foo = "bar"}

Implementation notes:
* JSON numbers become Lua numbers (doubles). Large integers may lose precision.
* JSON null becomes Lua nil.
* JSON arrays become Lua tables with integer keys starting at 1.
* JSON objects become Lua tables with string keys.
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Json (
    registerJsonModule,
) where

import qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson
import qualified Data.Aeson.Text as AesonText
import qualified Data.ByteString as BS
import Data.Scientific (Scientific, floatingOrInteger)
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Lazy as LazyText
import qualified Data.Vector as Vector
import qualified HsLua as Lua

import qualified Data.Aeson.KeyMap as KeyMap
import qualified Data.Aeson.Key as AesonKey

-- | Register the json module in the Lua state.
registerJsonModule :: Lua.State -> IO ()
registerJsonModule lstate = Lua.runWith lstate $ do
    Lua.newtable  -- Create module table

    -- json.encode(value) -> string
    Lua.pushName "encode"
    pushHaskellFn luaEncode
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

    -- json.decode(string) -> value
    Lua.pushName "decode"
    pushHaskellFn luaDecode
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

    -- Register as 'json' module
    Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "json")
  where
    pushHaskellFn :: (forall e. Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults) -> Lua.Lua ()
    pushHaskellFn fn = do
        -- Create a Haskell function wrapper
        Lua.pushpreparedfunction fn

-- | Encode a Lua value to JSON string.
-- Lua: json.encode(value) -> string
luaEncode :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaEncode = do
    -- Convert Lua value to Aeson Value, then to string
    jsonVal <- luaToAesonValue
    let txt = LazyText.toStrict $ AesonText.encodeToLazyText jsonVal
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 txt)
    pure 1

-- | Decode a JSON string to a Lua value.
-- Lua: json.decode(string) -> value or nil, error
luaDecode :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaDecode = do
    -- Get string argument
    str <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 1
    case Aeson.eitherDecodeStrict' str of
        Left err -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack err)
            pure 2  -- nil, error
        Right val -> do
            aesonToLuaValue val
            pure 1

-- | Convert Lua value at top of stack to Aeson Value.
luaToAesonValue :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
luaToAesonValue = do
    ltype <- Lua.ltype (Lua.nthTop 1)
    case ltype of
        Lua.TypeNil -> do
            Lua.pop 1
            pure Aeson.Null
        Lua.TypeBoolean -> do
            b <- Lua.toboolean (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.Bool b
        Lua.TypeNumber -> do
            mNum <- Lua.tonumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            case mNum of
                Nothing -> pure Aeson.Null
                Just n -> pure $ Aeson.Number $ fromFloatDigits n
        Lua.TypeString -> do
            s <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.String (Text.decodeUtf8 s)
        Lua.TypeTable -> do
            convertTable
        _ -> do
            -- For other types, convert to string
            str <- luaValueToString
            pure $ Aeson.String str
  where
    fromFloatDigits :: Double -> Scientific
    fromFloatDigits = read . show

    luaValueToString :: Lua.LuaE e Text
    luaValueToString = do
        Lua.pushglobaltable
        Lua.pushName (Lua.Name "tostring")
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)
        _ <- Lua.pcall 1 1 Nothing
        s <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
        Lua.pop 1
        pure $ Text.decodeUtf8 s

-- | Convert Lua table to Aeson Value (object or array).
convertTable :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertTable = do
    isArr <- isArray
    if isArr
        then convertArray
        else convertObject

-- | Check if table at top of stack is an array.
-- An array has sequential integer keys starting at 1.
isArray :: Lua.LuaE e Bool
isArray = do
    Lua.pushnil
    isSequential 1
  where
    isSequential :: Int -> Lua.LuaE e Bool
    isSequential expectedIdx = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then pure True
            else do
                isNum <- Lua.isnumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
                if isNum
                    then do
                        mIdx <- Lua.tointeger (Lua.nthTop 1)
                        Lua.pop 2
                        case mIdx of
                            Just idx
                                | idx == fromIntegral expectedIdx ->
                                    isSequential (expectedIdx + 1)
                            _ -> pure False
                    else do
                        Lua.pop 2
                        pure False

-- | Convert array table to JSON Array.
convertArray :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertArray = do
    vals <- collectArrayValues
    pure $ Aeson.Array (Vector.fromList vals)

-- | Collect values from array table.
collectArrayValues :: Lua.LuaE e [Aeson.Value]
collectArrayValues = do
    len' <- Lua.rawlen (Lua.nthTop 1)
    vals <- mapM (\i -> do
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        val <- luaToAesonValue
        pure val
        ) [1 .. fromIntegral len']
    Lua.pop 1  -- pop the table
    pure vals

-- | Convert object table to JSON Object.
convertObject :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertObject = do
    pairs <- collectObjectPairs
    pure $ Aeson.Object (KeyMap.fromList pairs)

-- | Collect key-value pairs from object table.
collectObjectPairs :: Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
collectObjectPairs = do
    Lua.pushnil
    go []
  where
    go :: [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)] -> Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
    go acc = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then do
                Lua.pop 1  -- pop the table
                pure acc
            else do
                key <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
                val <- luaToAesonValue
                Lua.pop 1  -- pop key
                go ((AesonKey.fromText (Text.decodeUtf8 key), val) : acc)

-- | Convert Aeson Value to Lua value and push onto stack.
aesonToLuaValue :: Aeson.Value -> Lua.LuaE e ()
aesonToLuaValue Aeson.Null = Lua.pushnil
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Bool b) = Lua.pushboolean b
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Number n) = 
    case floatingOrInteger n of
        Left d -> Lua.pushnumber d
        Right i -> Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.String t) = Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 t)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Array arr) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(i, val) -> do
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral (i + 1))  -- key (1-indexed)
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)  -- move key below value
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (zip [0..] (Vector.toList arr))
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Object obj) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(k, val) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ AesonKey.toText k)
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (KeyMap.toList obj)
"}

message.reasoning_content:
I see the issue - HsLua 2.3 API is different. Let me check the actual HsLua API and use the correct functions. Looking at the errors, I need to:

1. Use `Lua.pushHaskellFunction` instead of `pushDocumentedFunction` (or check the actual API)
2. Fix `seti` usage - use `rawseti` or set table entries differently
3. Fix `StackIndex` type issues

Let me simplify and use the basic HsLua API correctly:

    pure $ Aeson.Object (KeyMap.fromList pairs)

-- | Collect key-value pairs from object table.

    Lua.pushnil
    go []
  where
    go :: [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)] -> Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
    go acc = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)

                key <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)

aesonToLuaValue :: Aeson.Value -> Lua.LuaE e ()

You are an expert Haskell and Bash programmer specialized in writing clean, idiomatic, and efficient Haskell code.
When writing Bash code:

Use list-files to explore the project structure, read-file to understand existing code, write-file to create or modify files, and check-code to verify your work.

{"filepath": "./src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox/Modules/Json.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}
{-# LANGUAGE ScopedTypeVariables #-}

{- | JSON module for LuaToolbox.

Provides JSON encoding and decoding functions to Lua scripts:

> local json = require("json")
> local encoded = json.encode({foo = "bar", count = 42})
> -- encoded: '{"foo":"bar","count":42}'
> local decoded = json.decode('{"foo":"bar"}')
> -- decoded: {foo = "bar"}

Implementation notes:
* JSON numbers become Lua numbers (doubles). Large integers may lose precision.
* JSON null becomes Lua nil.
* JSON arrays become Lua tables with integer keys starting at 1.
* JSON objects become Lua tables with string keys.
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Json (
    registerJsonModule,
) where

import qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson
import qualified Data.Aeson.Text as AesonText
import qualified Data.ByteString as BS
import Data.Scientific (Scientific, floatingOrInteger)
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Lazy as LazyText
import qualified Data.Vector as Vector
import qualified HsLua as Lua
import qualified HsLua.Marshalling as Lua

import qualified Data.Aeson.KeyMap as KeyMap
import qualified Data.Aeson.Key as AesonKey

-- | Register the json module in the Lua state.
registerJsonModule :: Lua.State -> IO ()
registerJsonModule lstate = Lua.runWith lstate $ do
    Lua.newtable  -- Create module table

    -- json.encode(value) -> string
    Lua.pushName "encode"
    Lua.pushDocumentedFunction Lua.FnDocumentation
        { Lua.fnName = "encode"
        , Lua.fnDescription = "Encode a Lua value to JSON string"
        , Lua.fnArgs = ["value"]
        , Lua.fnResults = ["json_string"]
        } luaEncode
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

    -- json.decode(string) -> value
    Lua.pushName "decode"
    Lua.pushDocumentedFunction Lua.FnDocumentation
        { Lua.fnName = "decode"
        , Lua.fnDescription = "Decode a JSON string to a Lua value"
        , Lua.fnArgs = ["json_string"]
        , Lua.fnResults = ["value", "error"]
        } luaDecode
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

    -- Register as 'json' module
    Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "json")

-- | Encode a Lua value to JSON string.
-- Lua: json.encode(value) -> string
luaEncode :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaEncode = do
    -- Convert Lua value to Aeson Value, then to string
    jsonVal <- luaToAesonValue
    let txt = LazyText.toStrict $ AesonText.encodeToLazyText jsonVal
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 txt)
    pure 1

-- | Decode a JSON string to a Lua value.
-- Lua: json.decode(string) -> value or nil, error
luaDecode :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaDecode = do
    -- Get string argument
    str <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 1
    case Aeson.eitherDecodeStrict' str of
        Left err -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack err)
            pure 2  -- nil, error
        Right val -> do
            aesonToLuaValue val
            pure 1

-- | Convert Lua value at top of stack to Aeson Value.
luaToAesonValue :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
luaToAesonValue = do
    ltype <- Lua.ltype (Lua.nthTop 1)
    case ltype of
        Lua.TypeNil -> do
            Lua.pop 1
            pure Aeson.Null
        Lua.TypeBoolean -> do
            b <- Lua.toboolean (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.Bool b
        Lua.TypeNumber -> do
            mNum <- Lua.tonumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            case mNum of
                Nothing -> pure Aeson.Null
                Just n -> pure $ Aeson.Number $ fromFloatDigits n
        Lua.TypeString -> do
            s <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.String (Text.decodeUtf8 s)
        Lua.TypeTable -> do
            convertTable
        _ -> do
            -- For other types, convert to string
            str <- luaValueToString
            pure $ Aeson.String str
  where
    fromFloatDigits :: Double -> Scientific
    fromFloatDigits = read . show

    luaValueToString :: Lua.LuaE e Text
    luaValueToString = do
        Lua.pushglobaltable
        Lua.pushName (Lua.Name "tostring")
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)
        _ <- Lua.pcall 1 1 Nothing
        s <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
        Lua.pop 1
        pure $ Text.decodeUtf8 s

convertTable :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertTable = do
    isArr <- isArray
    if isArr
        then convertArray
        else convertObject

-- | Check if table at top of stack is an array.
-- An array has sequential integer keys starting at 1.
isArray :: Lua.LuaE e Bool
isArray = do
    Lua.pushnil
    isSequential 1
  where
    isSequential :: Int -> Lua.LuaE e Bool
    isSequential expectedIdx = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then pure True
            else do
                isNum <- Lua.isnumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
                if isNum
                    then do
                        mIdx <- Lua.tointeger (Lua.nthTop 1)
                        Lua.pop 2
                        case mIdx of
                            Just idx
                                | idx == fromIntegral expectedIdx ->
                                    isSequential (expectedIdx + 1)
                            _ -> pure False
                    else do
                        Lua.pop 2
                        pure False

-- | Convert array table to JSON Array.
convertArray :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertArray = do
    vals <- collectArrayValues
    pure $ Aeson.Array (Vector.fromList vals)

-- | Collect values from array table.
collectArrayValues :: Lua.LuaE e [Aeson.Value]
collectArrayValues = do
    len' <- Lua.rawlen (Lua.nthTop 1)
    vals <- mapM (\i -> do
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        val <- luaToAesonValue
        pure val
        ) [1 .. fromIntegral len']
    Lua.pop 1  -- pop the table
    pure vals

-- | Convert object table to JSON Object.
convertObject :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertObject = do
    pairs <- collectObjectPairs
    pure $ Aeson.Object (KeyMap.fromList pairs)

-- | Collect key-value pairs from object table.
collectObjectPairs :: Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
collectObjectPairs = do
    Lua.pushnil
    go []
  where
    go :: [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)] -> Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
    go acc = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then do
                Lua.pop 1  -- pop the table
                pure acc
            else do
                key <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
                val <- luaToAesonValue
                Lua.pop 1  -- pop key
                go ((AesonKey.fromText (Text.decodeUtf8 key), val) : acc)

-- | Convert Aeson Value to Lua value and push onto stack.
aesonToLuaValue :: Aeson.Value -> Lua.LuaE e ()
aesonToLuaValue Aeson.Null = Lua.pushnil
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Bool b) = Lua.pushboolean b
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Number n) = 
    case floatingOrInteger n of
        Left d -> Lua.pushnumber d
        Right i -> Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.String t) = Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 t)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Array arr) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(i, val) -> do
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral (i + 1))  -- key (1-indexed)
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)  -- move key below value
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (zip [0..] (Vector.toList arr))
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Object obj) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(k, val) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ AesonKey.toText k)
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (KeyMap.toList obj)
"}

            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.String (Text.decodeUtf8 s)
        Lua.TypeTable -> do
            convertTable
        _ -> do
            -- For other types, convert to string
            str <- luaValueToString
            pure $ Aeson.String str
  where
    fromFloatDigits :: Double -> Scientific
    fromFloatDigits = read . show

    luaValueToString :: Lua.LuaE e Text
    luaValueToString = do
        Lua.pushglobaltable
        Lua.pushName (Lua.Name "tostring")
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)
        _ <- Lua.pcall 1 1 Nothing
        s <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
        Lua.pop 1
        pure $ Text.decodeUtf8 s

-- | Convert Lua table to Aeson Value (object or array).
convertTable :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertTable = do
    isArr <- isArray
    if isArr
        then convertArray
        else convertObject

-- | Check if table at top of stack is an array.
-- An array has sequential integer keys starting at 1.
isArray :: Lua.LuaE e Bool
isArray = do
    Lua.pushnil
    isSequential 1
  where
    isSequential :: Int -> Lua.LuaE e Bool
    isSequential expectedIdx = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then pure True
            else do
                isNum <- Lua.isnumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
                if isNum
                    then do
                        mIdx <- Lua.tointeger (Lua.nthTop 1)
                        Lua.pop 2
                        case mIdx of
                            Just idx
                                | idx == fromIntegral expectedIdx ->
                                    isSequential (expectedIdx + 1)
                            _ -> pure False
                    else do
                        Lua.pop 2
                        pure False

-- | Convert array table to JSON Array.
convertArray :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertArray = do
    vals <- collectArrayValues
    pure $ Aeson.Array (Vector.fromList vals)

-- | Collect values from array table.
collectArrayValues :: Lua.LuaE e [Aeson.Value]
collectArrayValues = do
    len' <- Lua.rawlen (Lua.nthTop 1)
    vals <- mapM (\i -> do
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        val <- luaToAesonValue
        pure val
        ) [1 .. fromIntegral len']
    Lua.pop 1  -- pop the table
    pure vals

-- | Convert object table to JSON Object.
convertObject :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertObject = do
    pairs <- collectObjectPairs
    pure $ Aeson.Object (KeyMap.fromList pairs)

-- | Collect key-value pairs from object table.
collectObjectPairs :: Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
collectObjectPairs = do
    Lua.pushnil
    go []
  where
    go :: [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)] -> Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
    go acc = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then do
                Lua.pop 1  -- pop the table
                pure acc
            else do
                key <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
                val <- luaToAesonValue
                Lua.pop 1  -- pop key
                go ((AesonKey.fromText (Text.decodeUtf8 key), val) : acc)

-- | Convert Aeson Value to Lua value and push onto stack.
aesonToLuaValue :: Aeson.Value -> Lua.LuaE e ()
aesonToLuaValue Aeson.Null = Lua.pushnil
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Bool b) = Lua.pushboolean b
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Number n) = 
    case floatingOrInteger n of
        Left d -> Lua.pushnumber d
        Right i -> Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.String t) = Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 t)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Array arr) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(i, val) -> do
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral (i + 1))  -- key (1-indexed)
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)  -- move key below value
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (zip [0..] (Vector.toList arr))
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Object obj) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(k, val) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ AesonKey.toText k)
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (KeyMap.toList obj)
"}

message.content: Let me check what HsLua modules are available:
message.reasoning_content:
It seems the existing code doesn't push functions yet. Let me look at the hslua package to understand how to properly create C functions. The issue is that `pushcfunction` expects a `FunPtr PreCFunction` which is a C function pointer, not a Haskell function.

In HsLua, to push a Haskell function as a Lua function, we need to use `pushHaskellFunction` or use the `preCFunction` wrapper.

Let me check the HsLua documentation. Based on the errors, I need to:
1. Use a different approach for pushing Haskell functions
2. Use `rawseti` instead of `seti`
3. Fix the type issues with `StackIndex` and `Number`

Let me look at the hslua-marshalling package which should provide helpers:

contents.userPrompt:
You are an expert Haskell and Bash programmer specialized in writing clean, idiomatic, and efficient Haskell code.
When writing Bash code:
- Avoid bash-specific logic but bash-ism are fine.
- Document required shell tools at the top of the bash script.
When writing Haskell code:
- Use modern Haskell practices (base >= 4.14)
- Prefer pure functions and explicit type signatures
- Use standard libraries when appropriate
- Add clear comments for complex logic
- Follow consistent formatting
Use list-files to explore the project structure, read-file to understand existing code, write-file to create or modify files, and check-code to verify your work.
Always compile code before considering the job done.
Your output format may be stored as commit message: hence the first line of your last message should be a summary line.

{"filepath": "./src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox/Modules/Http.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}
{-# LANGUAGE ScopedTypeVariables #-}

{- | HTTP client module for LuaToolbox.

Provides HTTP request functions with host validation:

> local http = require("http")
> local result = http.get("http://example.com/api", {headers = {Accept = "application/json"}})
> -- result: {status = 200, headers = {...}, body = "..."}
> 
> local result = http.post("http://example.com/api", '{"foo":"bar"}', {
>     headers = {["Content-Type"] = "application/json"}
> })

Host sandboxing: All hosts are validated against a whitelist. If the whitelist
is empty (default), NO hosts are allowed (secure default).

Note: HTTP operations are blocking. Lua coroutines can be used for
cooperative multitasking.

The http.request function provides full control over the request:
> local result = http.request({
>     method = "PUT",
>     url = "http://example.com/resource",
>     headers = {["Content-Type"] = "application/json"},
>     body = '{"key":"value"}'
> })
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Http (
    HttpConfig (..),
    registerHttpModule,
) where

import qualified Data.ByteString as BS
import Control.Exception (try, SomeException)
import Control.Monad.IO.Class (liftIO)
import Data.ByteString (ByteString)
import qualified Data.ByteString.Lazy as LBS
import Data.Maybe (fromMaybe)
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified HsLua as Lua
import Network.HTTP.Client (
    Manager,
    Request (..),
    RequestBody (RequestBodyLBS, RequestBodyBS),
    Response (..),
    httpLbs,
    parseRequest,
 )
import qualified Network.HTTP.Client.TLS as HTTPS
import Network.HTTP.Types (Status, statusCode)
import qualified Network.HTTP.Types.Header as Header

-- | HTTP module configuration with host validation.
data HttpConfig = HttpConfig
    { httpAllowedHosts :: [Text]
    -- ^ Whitelist of allowed hosts (e.g., ["localhost", "api.example.com"]).
    -- If empty (default), NO hosts are allowed (secure default).
    }
    deriving (Show, Eq)

-- | Register the http module in the Lua state.
-- Creates a new TLS manager for this module instance.
registerHttpModule :: Lua.State -> HttpConfig -> IO ()
registerHttpModule lstate config = do
    manager <- HTTPS.newTlsManager
    Lua.runWith lstate $ do
        Lua.newtable

        addFunction "get" (luaGet config manager)
        addFunction "post" (luaPost config manager)
        addFunction "request" (luaRequest config manager)

        Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "http")
  where
    addFunction :: BS.ByteString -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults -> Lua.LuaE e ()
    addFunction name fn = do
        Lua.pushName (Lua.Name name)
        Lua.pushcfunction fn
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

-- | Perform HTTP GET request.
-- Lua: http.get(url, options) -> response or nil, error
-- Options table: {headers = {key = value, ...}}
luaGet :: HttpConfig -> Manager -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaGet config manager = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop
    if nargs < 1
        then do
            Lua.pop nargs
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring "Usage: http.get(url, [options])"
            pure 2
        else do
            urlBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            -- Parse options if provided
            mOptions <- if nargs >= 2
                then do
                    -- Options table is below url on stack
                    Lua.pushnil
                    opts <- parseOptions (Lua.nthTop 2)
                    Lua.pop 1  -- pop nil
                    pure opts
                else pure (Just defaultOptions)
            Lua.pop nargs
            
            let url = Text.unpack $ Text.decodeUtf8 urlBs
            case validateUrl config url of
                Left err -> do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
                    pure 2
                Right validUrl -> do
                    let opts = fromMaybe defaultOptions mOptions
                    let reqBody = RequestBodyLBS LBS.empty
                    performRequest manager "GET" validUrl opts reqBody

-- | Perform HTTP POST request.
-- Lua: http.post(url, body, options) -> response or nil, error
-- Options table: {headers = {key = value, ...}}
luaPost :: HttpConfig -> Manager -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaPost config manager = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop
    if nargs < 2
        then do
            Lua.pop nargs
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring "Usage: http.post(url, body, [options])"
            pure 2
        else do
            urlBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
            bodyBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            -- Parse options if provided
            mOptions <- if nargs >= 3
                then do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    opts <- parseOptions (Lua.nthTop 3)
                    Lua.pop 1
                    pure opts
                else pure (Just defaultOptions)
            Lua.pop nargs
            
            let url = Text.unpack $ Text.decodeUtf8 urlBs
            case validateUrl config url of
                Left err -> do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
                    pure 2
                Right validUrl -> do
                    let opts = fromMaybe defaultOptions mOptions
                    let reqBody = RequestBodyBS bodyBs
                    performRequest manager "POST" validUrl opts reqBody

-- | Perform a generic HTTP request with full control.
-- Lua: http.request(options) -> response or nil, error
-- Options table: {
--     method = "GET" | "POST" | "PUT" | "DELETE" | ...,
--     url = "http://...",
--     headers = {key = value, ...},
--     body = "request body string"
-- }
luaRequest :: HttpConfig -> Manager -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaRequest config manager = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop
    if nargs < 1
        then do
            Lua.pop nargs
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring "Usage: http.request(options)"
            pure 2
        else do
            -- Get method
            Lua.pushstring "method"
            _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
            mMethodBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            
            -- Get url
            Lua.pushstring "url"
            _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
            urlBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            
            -- Get body (optional)
            Lua.pushstring "body"
            _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
            hasBody <- Lua.istype Lua.TypeString (Lua.nthTop 1)
            mBodyBs <- if hasBody
                then do
                    bs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
                    pure (Just bs)
                else pure Nothing
            Lua.pop 1
            
            -- Parse headers from options
            Lua.pushstring "headers"
            _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
            mOptions <- parseOptions (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            
            Lua.pop nargs
            
            let url = Text.unpack $ Text.decodeUtf8 urlBs
            case validateUrl config url of
                Left err -> do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
                    pure 2
                Right validUrl -> do
                    let opts = fromMaybe defaultOptions mOptions
                    let reqMethod = if BS.null mMethodBs then "GET" else Text.decodeUtf8 mMethodBs
                    let reqBody = case mBodyBs of
                            Just bs -> RequestBodyBS bs
                            Nothing -> RequestBodyLBS LBS.empty
                    performRequest manager (Text.unpack reqMethod) validUrl opts reqBody

-- | Request options.
data RequestOptions = RequestOptions
    { optHeaders :: [(ByteString, ByteString)]
    }

-- | Default request options.
defaultOptions :: RequestOptions
defaultOptions = RequestOptions []

-- | Parse options table from stack.
parseOptions :: Lua.StackIndex -> Lua.LuaE e (Maybe RequestOptions)
parseOptions idx = do
    isTable <- Lua.istype Lua.TypeTable idx
    if not isTable
        then pure Nothing
        else do
            Lua.pushnil
            headers <- parseHeaders idx []
            pure $ Just $ RequestOptions headers
  where
    parseHeaders :: Lua.StackIndex -> [(ByteString, ByteString)] -> Lua.LuaE e [(ByteString, ByteString)]
    parseHeaders tableIdx acc = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop $ stackIdxToInt tableIdx + 1)
        if not hasNext
            then pure acc
            else do
                keyBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
                valBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
                Lua.pop 1  -- keep key for next iteration
                if keyBs == "headers"
                    then do
                        -- Parse headers table
                        isTable <- Lua.istype Lua.TypeTable (Lua.nthTop 1)
                        if isTable
                            then do
                                hdrs <- parseHeadersTable (Lua.nthTop 1)
                                parseHeaders tableIdx (hdrs ++ acc)
                            else parseHeaders tableIdx acc
                    else parseHeaders tableIdx acc

    parseHeadersTable :: Lua.StackIndex -> Lua.LuaE e [(ByteString, ByteString)]
    parseHeadersTable idx' = do
        Lua.pushnil
        go []
      where
        go acc = do
            hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop $ stackIdxToInt idx' + 1)
            if not hasNext
                then pure acc
                else do
                    keyBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
                    valBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
                    Lua.pop 1
                    go ((keyBs, valBs) : acc)

stackIdxToInt :: Lua.StackIndex -> Int
stackIdxToInt (Lua.StackIndex n) = fromIntegral n

-- | Simple URL validation - extract host and check against whitelist.
validateUrl :: HttpConfig -> String -> Either Text String
validateUrl config url
    | null (httpAllowedHosts config) = Left "No hosts allowed"
    | otherwise = 
        case extractHost url of
            Nothing -> Left "Could not extract host from URL"
            Just host -> 
                if host `elem` httpAllowedHosts config
                    then Right url
                    else Left $ "Host not allowed: " <> host

-- | Extract host from URL (simple parser).
-- Expects format: http://host/... or https://host/... or http://host:port/...
extractHost :: String -> Maybe Text
extractHost url = 
    let url' = if "://" `Text.isInfixOf` Text.pack url
               then drop 1 $ dropWhile (/= '/') $ dropWhile (/= ':') $ dropWhile (/= '/') url
               else url
        hostPort = takeWhile (/= '/') url'
        host = takeWhile (/= ':') hostPort
    in if null host then Nothing else Just (Text.pack host)

-- | Perform HTTP request and return response table.
performRequest :: Manager -> String -> String -> RequestOptions -> RequestBody -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
performRequest manager reqMethod url opts reqBody = do
    result <- liftIO $ try $ do
        initReq <- parseRequest url
        let req = initReq
                { method = Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack reqMethod
                , requestBody = reqBody
                , requestHeaders = map (\(k, v) -> (Header.mk k, v)) (optHeaders opts)
                }
        httpLbs req manager
    
    case result of
        Left (e :: SomeException) -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
            pure 2
        Right response -> do
            pushResponse response
            pure 1

-- | Push HTTP response as Lua table.
pushResponse :: Response LBS.ByteString -> Lua.LuaE e ()
pushResponse response = do
    Lua.newtable
    
    -- status
    Lua.pushstring "status"
    Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral $ statusCode $ responseStatus response)
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
    
    -- headers
    Lua.pushstring "headers"
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(name, val) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.toLower $ Text.pack $ show name)
        Lua.pushstring val
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (responseHeaders response)
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
    
    -- body
    Lua.pushstring "body"
    Lua.pushstring (LBS.toStrict $ responseBody response)
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
"}

        initReq <- parseRequest url
        let req = initReq
                { method = Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack reqMethod
                , requestBody = reqBody
                , requestHeaders = map (\(k, v) -> (Header.mk k, v)) (optHeaders opts)
                }
        httpLbs req manager
    
    case result of
        Left (e :: SomeException) -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
            pure 2
        Right response -> do
            pushResponse response
            pure 1

-- | Push HTTP response as Lua table.
pushResponse :: Response LBS.ByteString -> Lua.LuaE e ()
pushResponse response = do
    Lua.newtable
    
    -- status
    Lua.pushstring "status"
    Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral $ statusCode $ responseStatus response)
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
    
    -- headers
    Lua.pushstring "headers"
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(name, val) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.toLower $ Text.pack $ show name)
        Lua.pushstring val
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (responseHeaders response)
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
    
    -- body
    Lua.pushstring "body"
    Lua.pushstring (LBS.toStrict $ responseBody response)
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
"}

function.arguments:
{"filepath": "./src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox/Modules/Http.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}
{-# LANGUAGE ScopedTypeVariables #-}

{- | HTTP client module for LuaToolbox.

Provides HTTP request functions with host validation:

> local http = require("http")
> local result = http.get("http://example.com/api", {headers = {Accept = "application/json"}})
> -- result: {status = 200, headers = {...}, body = "..."}
> 
> local result = http.post("http://example.com/api", '{"foo":"bar"}', {
>     headers = {["Content-Type"] = "application/json"}
> })

Host sandboxing: All hosts are validated against a whitelist. If the whitelist
is empty (default), NO hosts are allowed (secure default).

Note: HTTP operations are blocking. Lua coroutines can be used for
cooperative multitasking.

The http.request function provides full control over the request:
> local result = http.request({
>     method = "PUT",
>     url = "http://example.com/resource",
>     headers = {["Content-Type"] = "application/json"},
>     body = '{"key":"value"}'
> })
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Http (
    HttpConfig (..),
    registerHttpModule,
) where

import qualified Data.ByteString as BS
import Control.Exception (try, SomeException)
import Control.Monad.IO.Class (liftIO)
import Data.ByteString (ByteString)
import qualified Data.ByteString.Lazy as LBS
import Data.Maybe (fromMaybe)
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified HsLua as Lua
import Network.HTTP.Client (
    Manager,
    Request (..),
    RequestBody (RequestBodyLBS, RequestBodyBS),
    Response (..),
    httpLbs,
    parseRequest,
 )
import qualified Network.HTTP.Client.TLS as HTTPS
import Network.HTTP.Types (Status, statusCode)
import qualified Network.HTTP.Types.Header as Header

-- | HTTP module configuration with host validation.
data HttpConfig = HttpConfig
    { httpAllowedHosts :: [Text]
    -- ^ Whitelist of allowed hosts (e.g., ["localhost", "api.example.com"]).
    -- If empty (default), NO hosts are allowed (secure default).
    }
    deriving (Show, Eq)

-- | Register the http module in the Lua state.
-- Creates a new TLS manager for this module instance.
registerHttpModule :: Lua.State -> HttpConfig -> IO ()
registerHttpModule lstate config = do
    manager <- HTTPS.newTlsManager
    Lua.runWith lstate $ do
        Lua.newtable

        addFunction "get" (luaGet config manager)
        addFunction "post" (luaPost config manager)
        addFunction "request" (luaRequest config manager)

        Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "http")
  where
    addFunction :: BS.ByteString -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults -> Lua.LuaE e ()
    addFunction name fn = do
        Lua.pushName (Lua.Name name)
        Lua.pushcfunction fn
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

-- | Perform HTTP GET request.
-- Lua: http.get(url, options) -> response or nil, error
-- Options table: {headers = {key = value, ...}}
luaGet :: HttpConfig -> Manager -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaGet config manager = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop
    if nargs < 1
        then do
            Lua.pop nargs
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring "Usage: http.get(url, [options])"
            pure 2
        else do
            urlBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            -- Parse options if provided
            mOptions <- if nargs >= 2
                then do
                    -- Options table is below url on stack
                    Lua.pushnil
                    opts <- parseOptions (Lua.nthTop 2)
                    Lua.pop 1  -- pop nil
                    pure opts
                else pure (Just defaultOptions)
            Lua.pop nargs
            
            let url = Text.unpack $ Text.decodeUtf8 urlBs
            case validateUrl config url of
                Left err -> do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
                    pure 2
                Right validUrl -> do
                    let opts = fromMaybe defaultOptions mOptions
                    let reqBody = RequestBodyLBS LBS.empty
                    performRequest manager "GET" validUrl opts reqBody

-- | Perform HTTP POST request.
-- Lua: http.post(url, body, options) -> response or nil, error
-- Options table: {headers = {key = value, ...}}
luaPost :: HttpConfig -> Manager -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaPost config manager = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop
    if nargs < 2
        then do
            Lua.pop nargs
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring "Usage: http.post(url, body, [options])"
            pure 2
        else do
            urlBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
            bodyBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            -- Parse options if provided
            mOptions <- if nargs >= 3
                then do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    opts <- parseOptions (Lua.nthTop 3)
                    Lua.pop 1
                    pure opts
                else pure (Just defaultOptions)
            Lua.pop nargs
            
            let url = Text.unpack $ Text.decodeUtf8 urlBs
            case validateUrl config url of
                Left err -> do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
                    pure 2
                Right validUrl -> do
                    let opts = fromMaybe defaultOptions mOptions
                    let reqBody = RequestBodyBS bodyBs
                    performRequest manager "POST" validUrl opts reqBody

-- | Perform a generic HTTP request with full control.
-- Lua: http.request(options) -> response or nil, error
-- Options table: {
--     method = "GET" | "POST" | "PUT" | "DELETE" | ...,
--     url = "http://...",
--     headers = {key = value, ...},
--     body = "request body string"
-- }
luaRequest :: HttpConfig -> Manager -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaRequest config manager = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop
    if nargs < 1
        then do
            Lua.pop nargs
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring "Usage: http.request(options)"
            pure 2
        else do
            -- Get method
            Lua.pushstring "method"
            _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
            mMethodBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            
            -- Get url
            Lua.pushstring "url"
            _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
            urlBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            
            -- Get body (optional)
            Lua.pushstring "body"
            _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
            hasBody <- Lua.istype Lua.TypeString (Lua.nthTop 1)
            mBodyBs <- if hasBody
                then do
                    bs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
                    pure (Just bs)
                else pure Nothing
            Lua.pop 1
            
            -- Parse headers from options
            Lua.pushstring "headers"
            _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
            mOptions <- parseOptions (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            
            Lua.pop nargs
            
            let url = Text.unpack $ Text.decodeUtf8 urlBs
            case validateUrl config url of
                Left err -> do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
                    pure 2
                Right validUrl -> do
                    let opts = fromMaybe defaultOptions mOptions
                    let reqMethod = if BS.null mMethodBs then "GET" else Text.decodeUtf8 mMethodBs
                    let reqBody = case mBodyBs of
                            Just bs -> RequestBodyBS bs
                            Nothing -> RequestBodyLBS LBS.empty
                    performRequest manager (Text.unpack reqMethod) validUrl opts reqBody

-- | Request options.
data RequestOptions = RequestOptions
    { optHeaders :: [(ByteString, ByteString)]
    }

-- | Default request options.
defaultOptions :: RequestOptions
defaultOptions = RequestOptions []

-- | Parse options table from stack.
parseOptions :: Lua.StackIndex -> Lua.LuaE e (Maybe RequestOptions)
parseOptions idx = do
    isTable <- Lua.istype Lua.TypeTable idx
    if not isTable
        then pure Nothing
        else do
            Lua.pushnil
            headers <- parseHeaders idx []
            pure $ Just $ RequestOptions headers
  where
    parseHeaders :: Lua.StackIndex -> [(ByteString, ByteString)] -> Lua.LuaE e [(ByteString, ByteString)]
    parseHeaders tableIdx acc = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop $ stackIdxToInt tableIdx + 1)
        if not hasNext
            then pure acc
            else do
                keyBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
                valBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
                Lua.pop 1  -- keep key for next iteration
                if keyBs == "headers"
                    then do
                        -- Parse headers table
                        isTable <- Lua.istype Lua.TypeTable (Lua.nthTop 1)
                        if isTable
                            then do
                                hdrs <- parseHeadersTable (Lua.nthTop 1)
                                parseHeaders tableIdx (hdrs ++ acc)
                            else parseHeaders tableIdx acc
                    else parseHeaders tableIdx acc

    parseHeadersTable :: Lua.StackIndex -> Lua.LuaE e [(ByteString, ByteString)]
    parseHeadersTable idx' = do
        Lua.pushnil
        go []
      where
        go acc = do
            hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop $ stackIdxToInt idx' + 1)
            if not hasNext
                then pure acc
                else do
                    keyBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
                    valBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
                    Lua.pop 1
                    go ((keyBs, valBs) : acc)

stackIdxToInt :: Lua.StackIndex -> Int
stackIdxToInt (Lua.StackIndex n) = fromIntegral n

-- | Simple URL validation - extract host and check against whitelist.
validateUrl :: HttpConfig -> String -> Either Text String
validateUrl config url
    | null (httpAllowedHosts config) = Left "No hosts allowed"
    | otherwise = 
        case extractHost url of
            Nothing -> Left "Could not extract host from URL"
            Just host -> 
                if host `elem` httpAllowedHosts config
                    then Right url
                    else Left $ "Host not allowed: " <> host

-- | Extract host from URL (simple parser).
-- Expects format: http://host/... or https://host/... or http://host:port/...
extractHost :: String -> Maybe Text
extractHost url = 
    let url' = if "://" `Text.isInfixOf` Text.pack url
               then drop 1 $ dropWhile (/= '/') $ dropWhile (/= ':') $ dropWhile (/= '/') url
               else url
        hostPort = takeWhile (/= '/') url'
        host = takeWhile (/= ':') hostPort
    in if null host then Nothing else Just (Text.pack host)

-- | Perform HTTP request and return response table.
performRequest :: Manager -> String -> String -> RequestOptions -> RequestBody -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
performRequest manager reqMethod url opts reqBody = do
    result <- liftIO $ try $ do
        initReq <- parseRequest url
        let req = initReq
                { method = Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack reqMethod
                , requestBody = reqBody
                , requestHeaders = map (\(k, v) -> (Header.mk k, v)) (optHeaders opts)
                }
        httpLbs req manager
    
    case result of
        Left (e :: SomeException) -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
            pure 2
        Right response -> do
            pushResponse response
            pure 1

-- | Push HTTP response as Lua table.
pushResponse :: Response LBS.ByteString -> Lua.LuaE e ()
pushResponse response = do
    Lua.newtable
    
    -- status
    Lua.pushstring "status"
    Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral $ statusCode $ responseStatus response)
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
    
    -- headers
    Lua.pushstring "headers"
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(name, val) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.toLower $ Text.pack $ show name)
        Lua.pushstring val
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        ) (responseHeaders response)
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
    
    -- body
    Lua.pushstring "body"
    Lua.pushstring (LBS.toStrict $ responseBody response)
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
"}

function.arguments:
{"filepath": "./src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox/Modules/Fs.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}
{-# LANGUAGE ScopedTypeVariables #-}

{- | Sandboxed filesystem module for LuaToolbox.

Provides filesystem operations with path validation:

> local fs = require("fs")
> local content, err = fs.read("/path/to/file.txt")
> local ok, err = fs.write("/path/to/file.txt", "data")
> local exists = fs.exists("/path/to/file.txt")
> local files, err = fs.list("/path/to/dir")
> local ok, err = fs.mkdir("/path/to/dir")

Path sandboxing: All paths are validated against a whitelist of allowed paths.
Operations fail if the path is not within an allowed directory.

Note: Path validation uses simple prefix matching and may not catch all
edge cases (symlinks, path traversal attempts using '..', etc.).
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Fs (
    FsConfig (..),
    registerFsModule,
) where

import qualified Data.ByteString as BS
import Control.Exception (try, IOException)
import Control.Monad.IO.Class (liftIO)
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified HsLua as Lua
import System.Directory (
    createDirectoryIfMissing,
    doesDirectoryExist,
    doesFileExist,
    listDirectory,
 )
import System.FilePath (isAbsolute, normalise, (</>), takeDirectory, isPathSeparator)
import Data.List (isPrefixOf)

-- | Filesystem module configuration with path sandboxing.
data FsConfig = FsConfig
    { fsAllowedPaths :: [FilePath]
    -- ^ Whitelist of allowed paths. If empty, NO paths are allowed (secure default).
    }
    deriving (Show, Eq)

-- | Register the fs module in the Lua state.
registerFsModule :: Lua.State -> FsConfig -> IO ()
registerFsModule lstate config = Lua.runWith lstate $ do
    Lua.newtable

    addFunction "read" (luaRead config)
    addFunction "write" (luaWrite config)
    addFunction "exists" (luaExists config)
    addFunction "list" (luaList config)
    addFunction "mkdir" (luaMkdir config)
    addFunction "isdir" (luaIsDir config)
    addFunction "isfile" (luaIsFile config)

    Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "fs")
  where
    addFunction :: BS.ByteString -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults -> Lua.LuaE e ()
    addFunction name fn = do
        Lua.pushName (Lua.Name name)
        Lua.pushcfunction fn
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

-- | Read file contents.
-- Lua: fs.read(path) -> content or nil, error
luaRead :: FsConfig -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaRead config = do
    mPath <- getPathArg config 1
    case mPath of
        Left err -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
            pure 2
        Right validPath -> do
            result <- liftIO $ try $ BS.readFile validPath
            case result of
                Left (e :: IOException) -> do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
                    pure 2
                Right content -> do
                    Lua.pushstring content
                    pure 1

-- | Write file contents.
-- Lua: fs.write(path, content) -> true or false, error
luaWrite :: FsConfig -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaWrite config = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop
    if nargs < 2
        then do
            Lua.pop nargs
            Lua.pushboolean False
            Lua.pushstring "Usage: fs.write(path, content)"
            pure 2
        else do
            mPath <- getPathArg config (nargs - 1)
            content <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop nargs
            case mPath of
                Left err -> do
                    Lua.pushboolean False
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
                    pure 2
                Right validPath -> do
                    -- Ensure parent directory exists
                    let parentDir = takeDirectory validPath
                    parentResult <- liftIO $ try $ createDirectoryIfMissing True parentDir
                    case parentResult of
                        Left (e :: IOException) -> do
                            Lua.pushboolean False
                            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
                            pure 2
                        Right () -> do
                            result <- liftIO $ try $ BS.writeFile validPath content
                            case result of
                                Left (e :: IOException) -> do
                                    Lua.pushboolean False
                                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
                                    pure 2
                                Right () -> do
                                    Lua.pushboolean True
                                    pure 1

-- | Check if path exists.
-- Lua: fs.exists(path) -> boolean
luaExists :: FsConfig -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaExists config = do
    mPath <- getPathArg config 1
    case mPath of
        Left _ -> do
            Lua.pushboolean False
            pure 1
        Right validPath -> do
            fileExists <- liftIO $ doesFileExist validPath
            dirExists <- liftIO $ doesDirectoryExist validPath
            Lua.pushboolean (fileExists || dirExists)
            pure 1

-- | List directory contents.
-- Lua: fs.list(path) -> {names} or nil, error
luaList :: FsConfig -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaList config = do
    mPath <- getPathArg config 1
    case mPath of
        Left err -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
            pure 2
        Right validPath -> do
            isDir <- liftIO $ doesDirectoryExist validPath
            if not isDir
                then do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    Lua.pushstring "Not a directory"
                    pure 2
                else do
                    result <- liftIO $ try $ listDirectory validPath
                    case result of
                        Left (e :: IOException) -> do
                            Lua.pushnil
                            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
                            pure 2
                        Right entries -> do
                            pushStringList entries
                            pure 1

-- | Create directory.
-- Lua: fs.mkdir(path) -> true or false, error
luaMkdir :: FsConfig -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaMkdir config = do
    mPath <- getPathArg config 1
    case mPath of
        Left err -> do
            Lua.pushboolean False
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
            pure 2
        Right validPath -> do
            result <- liftIO $ try $ createDirectoryIfMissing True validPath
            case result of
                Left (e :: IOException) -> do
                    Lua.pushboolean False
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
                    pure 2
                Right () -> do
                    Lua.pushboolean True
                    pure 1

-- | Check if path is a directory.
-- Lua: fs.isdir(path) -> boolean
luaIsDir :: FsConfig -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaIsDir config = do
    mPath <- getPathArg config 1
    case mPath of
        Left _ -> do
            Lua.pushboolean False
            pure 1
        Right validPath -> do
            isDir <- liftIO $ doesDirectoryExist validPath
            Lua.pushboolean isDir
            pure 1

-- | Check if path is a file.
-- Lua: fs.isfile(path) -> boolean
luaIsFile :: FsConfig -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaIsFile config = do
    mPath <- getPathArg config 1
    case mPath of
        Left _ -> do
            Lua.pushboolean False
            pure 1
        Right validPath -> do
            isFile <- liftIO $ doesFileExist validPath
            Lua.pushboolean isFile
            pure 1

-- | Validate and normalize path against allowed paths.
validatePath :: FsConfig -> FilePath -> Either Text FilePath
validatePath config path
    | null (fsAllowedPaths config) = Left "No paths allowed"
    | not (isAbsolute normalised) = Left "Path must be absolute"
    | containsTraversal normalised = Left "Path contains directory traversal"
    | otherwise = 
        if any (`isPathPrefixOf` normalised) (fsAllowedPaths config)
            then Right normalised
            else Left "Path not in allowed paths"
  where
    normalised = normalise path
    
    -- Check for path traversal attempts
    containsTraversal :: FilePath -> Bool
    containsTraversal p = 
        let parts = splitPath p
        in ".." `elem` parts
    
    splitPath :: FilePath -> [String]
    splitPath = go []
      where
        go acc [] = reverse acc
        go acc (c:cs)
            | isPathSeparator c = go acc cs
            | otherwise = 
                let (part, rest) = break isPathSeparator (c:cs)
                in go (part : acc) rest

    -- Check if prefix is a proper path prefix
    isPathPrefixOf :: FilePath -> FilePath -> Bool
    isPathPrefixOf prefix p = 
        prefix == p || 
        (prefix `isPrefixOf` p && 
         (null dropPrefix || isPathSeparator (head dropPrefix)))
      where
        dropPrefix = drop (length prefix) p

-- | Helper to get and validate a path argument.
getPathArg :: FsConfig -> Int -> Lua.LuaE e (Either Text FilePath)
getPathArg config offset = do
    top <- Lua.gettop
    let idx = fromIntegral $ fromIntegral top - offset + 1
    bs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop idx)
    let path = Text.unpack $ Text.decodeUtf8 bs
    pure $ validatePath config path

-- | Push a list of strings as a Lua table.
pushStringList :: [String] -> Lua.LuaE e ()
pushStringList items = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(i, s) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack s)
        Lua.seti (Lua.nthTop 2) (fromIntegral i)
        ) (zip [1..] items)
"}
