{"filepath": "./src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox/Modules/Http.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}

{- | HTTP client module for LuaToolbox.

Provides HTTP request functions with host validation:

> local http = require("http")
> local result = http.get("http://example.com/api", {headers = {Accept = "application/json"}})
> -- result: {status = 200, headers = {...}, body = "..."}
> 
> local result = http.post("http://example.com/api", '{"foo":"bar"}', {
>     headers = {["Content-Type"] = "application/json"}
> })

Host sandboxing: All hosts are validated against a whitelist. If the whitelist
is empty (default), NO hosts are allowed (secure default).

Note: HTTP operations are blocking. Lua coroutines can be used for
cooperative multitasking.

The http.request function provides full control over the request:
> local result = http.request({
>     method = "PUT",
>     url = "http://example.com/resource",
>     headers = {["Content-Type"] = "application/json"},
>     body = '{"key":"value"}'
> })
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Http (
    HttpConfig (..),
    registerHttpModule,
) where

import Control.Exception (try, SomeException)
import Control.Monad.IO.Class (liftIO)
import Data.ByteString (ByteString)
import qualified Data.ByteString as BS
import qualified Data.ByteString.Lazy as LBS
import Data.Maybe (fromMaybe)
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified HsLua as Lua
import Network.HTTP.Client (
    Manager,
    Request (..),
    RequestBody (RequestBodyLBS, RequestBodyBS),
    Response (..),
    httpLbs,
    parseRequest,
    method,
 )
import qualified Network.HTTP.Client.TLS as HTTPS
import Network.HTTP.Types (Status, statusCode, renderStdMethod, parseMethod)
import qualified Network.HTTP.Types.Header as Header
import Network.URI (URI (..), URIAuth (..), parseURI)

-- | HTTP module configuration with host validation.
data HttpConfig = HttpConfig
    { httpAllowedHosts :: [Text]
    -- ^ Whitelist of allowed hosts (e.g., ["localhost", "api.example.com"]).
    -- If empty (default), NO hosts are allowed (secure default).
    }
    deriving (Show, Eq)

-- | Register the http module in the Lua state.
-- Creates a new TLS manager for this module instance.
registerHttpModule :: Lua.State -> HttpConfig -> IO ()
registerHttpModule lstate config = do
    manager <- HTTPS.newTlsManager
    Lua.runWith lstate $ do
        Lua.newtable

        addFunction "get" (luaGet config manager)
        addFunction "post" (luaPost config manager)
        addFunction "request" (luaRequest config manager)

        Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "http")
  where
    addFunction :: BS.ByteString -> Lua.HsFn e -> Lua.LuaE e ()
    addFunction name fn = do
        Lua.pushName (Lua.Name name)
        Lua.pushhsfunction fn
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthFromTop 3)

-- | Perform HTTP GET request.
-- Lua: http.get(url, options) -> response or nil, error
-- Options table: {headers = {key = value, ...}}
luaGet :: HttpConfig -> Manager -> Lua.HsFn e
luaGet config manager = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop
    if nargs < 1
        then do
            Lua.pop nargs
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring "Usage: http.get(url, [options])"
            pure 2
        else do
            urlBs <- Lua.tostring' Lua.stackTop
            -- Parse options if provided
            mOptions <- if nargs >= 2
                then do
                    -- Options table is below url on stack
                    Lua.pushnil
                    opts <- parseOptions (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
                    Lua.pop 1  -- pop nil
                    pure opts
                else pure (Just defaultOptions)
            Lua.pop nargs
            
            let url = Text.unpack $ Text.decodeUtf8 urlBs
            case validateUrl config url of
                Left err -> do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
                    pure 2
                Right validUrl -> do
                    let opts = fromMaybe defaultOptions mOptions
                    let reqBody = RequestBodyLBS LBS.empty
                    performRequest manager "GET" validUrl opts reqBody

-- | Perform HTTP POST request.
-- Lua: http.post(url, body, options) -> response or nil, error
-- Options table: {headers = {key = value, ...}}
luaPost :: HttpConfig -> Manager -> Lua.HsFn e
luaPost config manager = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop
    if nargs < 2
        then do
            Lua.pop nargs
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring "Usage: http.post(url, body, [options])"
            pure 2
        else do
            urlBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
            bodyBs <- Lua.tostring' Lua.stackTop
            -- Parse options if provided
            mOptions <- if nargs >= 3
                then do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    opts <- parseOptions (Lua.nthFromTop 3)
                    Lua.pop 1
                    pure opts
                else pure (Just defaultOptions)
            Lua.pop nargs
            
            let url = Text.unpack $ Text.decodeUtf8 urlBs
            case validateUrl config url of
                Left err -> do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
                    pure 2
                Right validUrl -> do
                    let opts = fromMaybe defaultOptions mOptions
                    let reqBody = RequestBodyBS bodyBs
                    performRequest manager "POST" validUrl opts reqBody

-- | Perform a generic HTTP request with full control.
-- Lua: http.request(options) -> response or nil, error
-- Options table: {
--     method = "GET" | "POST" | "PUT" | "DELETE" | ...,
--     url = "http://...",
--     headers = {key = value, ...},
--     body = "request body string"
-- }
luaRequest :: HttpConfig -> Manager -> Lua.HsFn e
luaRequest config manager = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop
    if nargs < 1
        then do
            Lua.pop nargs
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring "Usage: http.request(options)"
            pure 2
        else do
            -- Get method
            Lua.pushstring "method"
            _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
            mMethodBs <- Lua.tostring' Lua.stackTop
            Lua.pop 1
            
            -- Get url
            Lua.pushstring "url"
            _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
            urlBs <- Lua.tostring' Lua.stackTop
            Lua.pop 1
            
            -- Get body (optional)
            Lua.pushstring "body"
            _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
            hasBody <- Lua.istype Lua.TypeString Lua.stackTop
            mBodyBs <- if hasBody
                then do
                    bs <- Lua.tostring' Lua.stackTop
                    pure (Just bs)
                else pure Nothing
            Lua.pop 1
            
            -- Parse headers from options
            Lua.pushstring "headers"
            _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
            mOptions <- parseOptions Lua.stackTop
            Lua.pop 1
            
            Lua.pop nargs
            
            let url = Text.unpack $ Text.decodeUtf8 urlBs
            case validateUrl config url of
                Left err -> do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
                    pure 2
                Right validUrl -> do
                    let opts = fromMaybe defaultOptions mOptions
                    let reqMethod = if BS.null mMethodBs then "GET" else Text.decodeUtf8 mMethodBs
                    let reqBody = case mBodyBs of
                            Just bs -> RequestBodyBS bs
                            Nothing -> RequestBodyLBS LBS.empty
                    performRequest manager (Text.unpack reqMethod) validUrl opts reqBody

-- | Request options.
data RequestOptions = RequestOptions
    { optHeaders :: [(ByteString, ByteString)]
    }

-- | Default request options.
defaultOptions :: RequestOptions
defaultOptions = RequestOptions []

-- | Parse options table from stack.
parseOptions :: Lua.StackIndex -> Lua.LuaE e (Maybe RequestOptions)
parseOptions idx = do
    isTable <- Lua.istype Lua.TypeTable idx
    if not isTable
        then pure Nothing
        else do
            Lua.pushnil
            headers <- parseHeaders idx []
            pure $ Just $ RequestOptions headers
  where
    parseHeaders :: Lua.StackIndex -> [(ByteString, ByteString)] -> Lua.LuaE e [(ByteString, ByteString)]
    parseHeaders tableIdx acc = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthFromTop $ stackIdxToInt tableIdx + 1)
        if not hasNext
            then pure acc
            else do
                keyBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
                valBs <- Lua.tostring' Lua.stackTop
                Lua.pop 1  -- keep key for next iteration
                if keyBs == "headers"
                    then do
                        -- Parse headers table
                        isTable <- Lua.istype Lua.TypeTable (Lua.nthFromTop 1)
                        if isTable
                            then do
                                hdrs <- parseHeadersTable (Lua.nthFromTop 1)
                                parseHeaders tableIdx (hdrs ++ acc)
                            else parseHeaders tableIdx acc
                    else parseHeaders tableIdx acc

    parseHeadersTable :: Lua.StackIndex -> Lua.LuaE e [(ByteString, ByteString)]
    parseHeadersTable idx' = do
        Lua.pushnil
        go []
      where
        go acc = do
            hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthFromTop $ stackIdxToInt idx' + 1)
            if not hasNext
                then pure acc
                else do
                    keyBs <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
                    valBs <- Lua.tostring' Lua.stackTop
                    Lua.pop 1
                    go ((keyBs, valBs) : acc)

stackIdxToInt :: Lua.StackIndex -> Int
stackIdxToInt (Lua.StackIndex n) = fromIntegral n

-- | Validate URL against allowed hosts.
validateUrl :: HttpConfig -> String -> Either Text String
validateUrl config url
    | null (httpAllowedHosts config) = Left "No hosts allowed"
    | otherwise = case parseURI url of
        Nothing -> Left "Invalid URL"
        Just uri -> case uriAuthority uri of
            Nothing -> Left "URL must have a host"
            Just auth -> 
                let host = Text.pack $ uriRegName auth
                in if host `elem` httpAllowedHosts config
                    then Right url
                    else Left $ "Host not allowed: " <> host

-- | Perform HTTP request and return response table.
performRequest :: Manager -> String -> String -> RequestOptions -> RequestBody -> Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
performRequest manager reqMethod url opts reqBody = do
    result <- liftIO $ try $ do
        initReq <- parseRequest url
        let req = initReq
                { method = encodeMethod reqMethod
                , requestBody = reqBody
                , requestHeaders = map (\(k, v) -> (Header.mk k, v)) (optHeaders opts)
                }
        httpLbs req manager
    
    case result of
        Left (e :: SomeException) -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
            pure 2
        Right response -> do
            pushResponse response
            pure 1
  where
    encodeMethod :: String -> ByteString
    encodeMethod m = case parseMethod (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack m) of
        Left _ -> renderStdMethod (parseMethodFallback m)
        Right stdMethod -> renderStdMethod stdMethod
    
    parseMethodFallback :: String -> Header.StdMethod
    parseMethodFallback m = case Text.toUpper (Text.pack m) of
        "GET" -> Header.GET
        "POST" -> Header.POST
        "PUT" -> Header.PUT
        "DELETE" -> Header.DELETE
        "HEAD" -> Header.HEAD
        "OPTIONS" -> Header.OPTIONS
        "PATCH" -> Header.PATCH
        _ -> Header.GET

-- | Push HTTP response as Lua table.
pushResponse :: Response LBS.ByteString -> Lua.LuaE e ()
pushResponse response = do
    Lua.newtable
    
    -- status
    Lua.pushstring "status"
    Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral $ statusCode $ responseStatus response)
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthFromTop 3)
    
    -- headers
    Lua.pushstring "headers"
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(name, val) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.toLower $ Text.pack $ show name)
        Lua.pushstring val
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthFromTop 3)
        ) (responseHeaders response)
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthFromTop 3)
    
    -- body
    Lua.pushstring "body"
    Lua.pushstring (LBS.toStrict $ responseBody response)
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthFromTop 3)
"}

function.arguments:
{"filepath": "./src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox/Modules/Fs.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}

{- | Sandboxed filesystem module for LuaToolbox.

Provides filesystem operations with path validation:

> local fs = require("fs")
> local content, err = fs.read("/path/to/file.txt")
> local ok, err = fs.write("/path/to/file.txt", "data")
> local exists = fs.exists("/path/to/file.txt")
> local files, err = fs.list("/path/to/dir")
> local ok, err = fs.mkdir("/path/to/dir")

Path sandboxing: All paths are validated against a whitelist of allowed paths.
Operations fail if the path is not within an allowed directory.

Note: Path validation uses simple prefix matching and may not catch all
edge cases (symlinks, path traversal attempts using '..', etc.).
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Fs (
    FsConfig (..),
    registerFsModule,
) where

import Control.Exception (try, IOException)
import Control.Monad.IO.Class (liftIO)
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified Data.ByteString as BS
import qualified HsLua as Lua
import System.Directory (
    createDirectoryIfMissing,
    doesDirectoryExist,
    doesFileExist,
    listDirectory,
 )
import System.FilePath (isAbsolute, normalise, (</>), takeDirectory, isPathSeparator)
import Data.List (isPrefixOf)

-- | Filesystem module configuration with path sandboxing.
data FsConfig = FsConfig
    { fsAllowedPaths :: [FilePath]
    -- ^ Whitelist of allowed paths. If empty, NO paths are allowed (secure default).
    }
    deriving (Show, Eq)

-- | Register the fs module in the Lua state.
registerFsModule :: Lua.State -> FsConfig -> IO ()
registerFsModule lstate config = Lua.runWith lstate $ do
    Lua.newtable

    addFunction "read" (luaRead config)
    addFunction "write" (luaWrite config)
    addFunction "exists" (luaExists config)
    addFunction "list" (luaList config)
    addFunction "mkdir" (luaMkdir config)
    addFunction "isdir" (luaIsDir config)
    addFunction "isfile" (luaIsFile config)

    Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "fs")
  where
    addFunction :: BS.ByteString -> Lua.HsFn e -> Lua.LuaE e ()
    addFunction name fn = do
        Lua.pushName (Lua.Name name)
        Lua.pushhsfunction fn
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthFromTop 3)

-- | Read file contents.
-- Lua: fs.read(path) -> content or nil, error
luaRead :: FsConfig -> Lua.HsFn e
luaRead config = do
    mPath <- getPathArg config 1
    case mPath of
        Left err -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
            pure 2
        Right validPath -> do
            result <- liftIO $ try $ BS.readFile validPath
            case result of
                Left (e :: IOException) -> do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
                    pure 2
                Right content -> do
                    Lua.pushstring content
                    pure 1

-- | Write file contents.
-- Lua: fs.write(path, content) -> true or false, error
luaWrite :: FsConfig -> Lua.HsFn e
luaWrite config = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop
    if nargs < 2
        then do
            Lua.pop nargs
            Lua.pushboolean False
            Lua.pushstring "Usage: fs.write(path, content)"
            pure 2
        else do
            mPath <- getPathArg config 1
            content <- Lua.tostring' Lua.stackTop
            Lua.pop nargs
            case mPath of
                Left err -> do
                    Lua.pushboolean False
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
                    pure 2
                Right validPath -> do
                    -- Ensure parent directory exists
                    let parentDir = takeDirectory validPath
                    parentResult <- liftIO $ try $ createDirectoryIfMissing True parentDir
                    case parentResult of
                        Left (e :: IOException) -> do
                            Lua.pushboolean False
                            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
                            pure 2
                        Right () -> do
                            result <- liftIO $ try $ BS.writeFile validPath content
                            case result of
                                Left (e :: IOException) -> do
                                    Lua.pushboolean False
                                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
                                    pure 2
                                Right () -> do
                                    Lua.pushboolean True
                                    pure 1

-- | Check if path exists.
-- Lua: fs.exists(path) -> boolean
luaExists :: FsConfig -> Lua.HsFn e
luaExists config = do
    mPath <- getPathArg config 1
    case mPath of
        Left _ -> do
            Lua.pushboolean False
            pure 1
        Right validPath -> do
            fileExists <- liftIO $ doesFileExist validPath
            dirExists <- liftIO $ doesDirectoryExist validPath
            Lua.pushboolean (fileExists || dirExists)
            pure 1

-- | List directory contents.
-- Lua: fs.list(path) -> {names} or nil, error
luaList :: FsConfig -> Lua.HsFn e
luaList config = do
    mPath <- getPathArg config 1
    case mPath of
        Left err -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
            pure 2
        Right validPath -> do
            isDir <- liftIO $ doesDirectoryExist validPath
            if not isDir
                then do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    Lua.pushstring "Not a directory"
                    pure 2
                else do
                    result <- liftIO $ try $ listDirectory validPath
                    case result of
                        Left (e :: IOException) -> do
                            Lua.pushnil
                            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
                            pure 2
                        Right entries -> do
                            pushStringList entries
                            pure 1

-- | Create directory.
-- Lua: fs.mkdir(path) -> true or false, error
luaMkdir :: FsConfig -> Lua.HsFn e
luaMkdir config = do
    mPath <- getPathArg config 1
    case mPath of
        Left err -> do
            Lua.pushboolean False
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
            pure 2
        Right validPath -> do
            result <- liftIO $ try $ createDirectoryIfMissing True validPath
            case result of
                Left (e :: IOException) -> do
                    Lua.pushboolean False
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
                    pure 2
                Right () -> do
                    Lua.pushboolean True
                    pure 1

-- | Check if path is a directory.
-- Lua: fs.isdir(path) -> boolean
luaIsDir :: FsConfig -> Lua.HsFn e
luaIsDir config = do
    mPath <- getPathArg config 1
    case mPath of
        Left _ -> do
            Lua.pushboolean False
            pure 1
        Right validPath -> do
            isDir <- liftIO $ doesDirectoryExist validPath
            Lua.pushboolean isDir
            pure 1

-- | Check if path is a file.
-- Lua: fs.isfile(path) -> boolean
luaIsFile :: FsConfig -> Lua.HsFn e
luaIsFile config = do
    mPath <- getPathArg config 1
    case mPath of
        Left _ -> do
            Lua.pushboolean False
            pure 1
        Right validPath -> do
            isFile <- liftIO $ doesFileExist validPath
            Lua.pushboolean isFile
            pure 1

-- | Validate and normalize path against allowed paths.
validatePath :: FsConfig -> FilePath -> Either Text FilePath
validatePath config path
    | null (fsAllowedPaths config) = Left "No paths allowed"
    | not (isAbsolute normalised) = Left "Path must be absolute"
    | containsTraversal normalised = Left "Path contains directory traversal"
    | otherwise = 
        if any (`isPathPrefixOf` normalised) (fsAllowedPaths config)
            then Right normalised
            else Left "Path not in allowed paths"
  where
    normalised = normalise path
    
    -- Check for path traversal attempts
    containsTraversal :: FilePath -> Bool
    containsTraversal p = 
        let parts = splitPath p
        in ".." `elem` parts
    
    splitPath :: FilePath -> [String]
    splitPath = go []
      where
        go acc [] = reverse acc
        go acc (c:cs)
            | isPathSeparator c = go acc cs
            | otherwise = 
                let (part, rest) = break isPathSeparator (c:cs)
                in go (part : acc) rest

    -- Check if prefix is a proper path prefix
    isPathPrefixOf :: FilePath -> FilePath -> Bool
    isPathPrefixOf prefix p = 
        prefix == p || 
        (prefix `isPrefixOf` p && 
         (null dropPrefix || isPathSeparator (head dropPrefix)))
      where
        dropPrefix = drop (length prefix) p

-- | Helper to get and validate a path argument.
getPathArg :: FsConfig -> Lua.StackIndex -> Lua.LuaE e (Either Text FilePath)
getPathArg config idx = do
    bs <- Lua.tostring' idx
    let path = Text.unpack $ Text.decodeUtf8 bs
    pure $ validatePath config path

-- | Push a list of strings as a Lua table.
pushStringList :: [String] -> Lua.LuaE e ()
pushStringList items = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(i, s) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack s)
        Lua.seti (Lua.nthFromTop 2) (fromIntegral i)
        ) (zip [1..] items)
"}

{"filepath": "./src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox/Modules/Fs.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}

{- | Sandboxed filesystem module for LuaToolbox.

Provides filesystem operations with path validation:

> local fs = require("fs")
> local content, err = fs.read("/path/to/file.txt")
> local ok, err = fs.write("/path/to/file.txt", "data")
> local exists = fs.exists("/path/to/file.txt")
> local files, err = fs.list("/path/to/dir")
> local ok, err = fs.mkdir("/path/to/dir")

Path sandboxing: All paths are validated against a whitelist of allowed paths.
Operations fail if the path is not within an allowed directory.

Note: Path validation uses simple prefix matching and may not catch all
edge cases (symlinks, path traversal attempts using '..', etc.).
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Fs (
    FsConfig (..),
    registerFsModule,
) where

import Control.Exception (try, IOException)
import Control.Monad.IO.Class (liftIO)
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified Data.ByteString as BS
import qualified HsLua as Lua
import System.Directory (
    createDirectoryIfMissing,
    doesDirectoryExist,
    doesFileExist,
    listDirectory,
 )
import System.FilePath (isAbsolute, normalise, (</>), takeDirectory, isPathSeparator)
import Data.List (isPrefixOf)

-- | Filesystem module configuration with path sandboxing.
data FsConfig = FsConfig
    { fsAllowedPaths :: [FilePath]
    -- ^ Whitelist of allowed paths. If empty, NO paths are allowed (secure default).
    }
    deriving (Show, Eq)

-- | Register the fs module in the Lua state.
registerFsModule :: Lua.State -> FsConfig -> IO ()
registerFsModule lstate config = Lua.runWith lstate $ do
    Lua.newtable

    addFunction "read" (luaRead config)
    addFunction "write" (luaWrite config)
    addFunction "exists" (luaExists config)
    addFunction "list" (luaList config)
    addFunction "mkdir" (luaMkdir config)
    addFunction "isdir" (luaIsDir config)
    addFunction "isfile" (luaIsFile config)

    Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "fs")
  where
    addFunction :: BS.ByteString -> Lua.HsFn e -> Lua.LuaE e ()
    addFunction name fn = do
        Lua.pushName (Lua.Name name)
        Lua.pushhsfunction fn
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthFromTop 3)

-- | Read file contents.
-- Lua: fs.read(path) -> content or nil, error
luaRead :: FsConfig -> Lua.HsFn e
luaRead config = do
    mPath <- getPathArg config 1
    case mPath of
        Left err -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
            pure 2
        Right validPath -> do
            result <- liftIO $ try $ BS.readFile validPath
            case result of
                Left (e :: IOException) -> do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
                    pure 2
                Right content -> do
                    Lua.pushstring content
                    pure 1

-- | Write file contents.
-- Lua: fs.write(path, content) -> true or false, error
luaWrite :: FsConfig -> Lua.HsFn e
luaWrite config = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop
    if nargs < 2
        then do
            Lua.pop nargs
            Lua.pushboolean False
            Lua.pushstring "Usage: fs.write(path, content)"
            pure 2
        else do
            mPath <- getPathArg config 1
            content <- Lua.tostring' Lua.stackTop
            Lua.pop nargs
            case mPath of
                Left err -> do
                    Lua.pushboolean False
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
                    pure 2
                Right validPath -> do
                    -- Ensure parent directory exists
                    let parentDir = takeDirectory validPath
                    parentResult <- liftIO $ try $ createDirectoryIfMissing True parentDir
                    case parentResult of
                        Left (e :: IOException) -> do
                            Lua.pushboolean False
                            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
                            pure 2
                        Right () -> do
                            result <- liftIO $ try $ BS.writeFile validPath content
                            case result of
                                Left (e :: IOException) -> do
                                    Lua.pushboolean False
                                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
                                    pure 2
                                Right () -> do
                                    Lua.pushboolean True
                                    pure 1

-- | Check if path exists.
-- Lua: fs.exists(path) -> boolean
luaExists :: FsConfig -> Lua.HsFn e
luaExists config = do
    mPath <- getPathArg config 1
    case mPath of
        Left _ -> do
            Lua.pushboolean False
            pure 1
        Right validPath -> do
            fileExists <- liftIO $ doesFileExist validPath
            dirExists <- liftIO $ doesDirectoryExist validPath
            Lua.pushboolean (fileExists || dirExists)
            pure 1

-- | List directory contents.
-- Lua: fs.list(path) -> {names} or nil, error
luaList :: FsConfig -> Lua.HsFn e
luaList config = do
    mPath <- getPathArg config 1
    case mPath of
        Left err -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
            pure 2
        Right validPath -> do
            isDir <- liftIO $ doesDirectoryExist validPath
            if not isDir
                then do
                    Lua.pushnil
                    Lua.pushstring "Not a directory"
                    pure 2
                else do
                    result <- liftIO $ try $ listDirectory validPath
                    case result of
                        Left (e :: IOException) -> do
                            Lua.pushnil
                            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
                            pure 2
                        Right entries -> do
                            pushStringList entries
                            pure 1

-- | Create directory.
-- Lua: fs.mkdir(path) -> true or false, error
luaMkdir :: FsConfig -> Lua.HsFn e
luaMkdir config = do
    mPath <- getPathArg config 1
    case mPath of
        Left err -> do
            Lua.pushboolean False
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 err)
            pure 2
        Right validPath -> do
            result <- liftIO $ try $ createDirectoryIfMissing True validPath
            case result of
                Left (e :: IOException) -> do
                    Lua.pushboolean False
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack $ show e)
                    pure 2
                Right () -> do
                    Lua.pushboolean True
                    pure 1

-- | Check if path is a directory.
-- Lua: fs.isdir(path) -> boolean
luaIsDir :: FsConfig -> Lua.HsFn e
luaIsDir config = do
    mPath <- getPathArg config 1
    case mPath of
        Left _ -> do
            Lua.pushboolean False
            pure 1
        Right validPath -> do
            isDir <- liftIO $ doesDirectoryExist validPath
            Lua.pushboolean isDir
            pure 1

-- | Check if path is a file.
-- Lua: fs.isfile(path) -> boolean
luaIsFile :: FsConfig -> Lua.HsFn e
luaIsFile config = do
    mPath <- getPathArg config 1
    case mPath of
        Left _ -> do
            Lua.pushboolean False
            pure 1
        Right validPath -> do
            isFile <- liftIO $ doesFileExist validPath
            Lua.pushboolean isFile
            pure 1

-- | Validate and normalize path against allowed paths.
validatePath :: FsConfig -> FilePath -> Either Text FilePath
validatePath config path
    | null (fsAllowedPaths config) = Left "No paths allowed"
    | not (isAbsolute normalised) = Left "Path must be absolute"
    | containsTraversal normalised = Left "Path contains directory traversal"
    | otherwise = 
        if any (`isPathPrefixOf` normalised) (fsAllowedPaths config)
            then Right normalised
            else Left "Path not in allowed paths"
  where
    normalised = normalise path
    
    -- Check for path traversal attempts
    containsTraversal :: FilePath -> Bool
    containsTraversal p = 
        let parts = splitPath p
        in ".." `elem` parts
    
    splitPath :: FilePath -> [String]
    splitPath = go []
      where
        go acc [] = reverse acc
        go acc (c:cs)
            | isPathSeparator c = go acc cs
            | otherwise = 
                let (part, rest) = break isPathSeparator (c:cs)
                in go (part : acc) rest

    -- Check if prefix is a proper path prefix
    isPathPrefixOf :: FilePath -> FilePath -> Bool
    isPathPrefixOf prefix p = 
        prefix == p || 
        (prefix `isPrefixOf` p && 
         (null dropPrefix || isPathSeparator (head dropPrefix)))
      where
        dropPrefix = drop (length prefix) p

-- | Helper to get and validate a path argument.
getPathArg :: FsConfig -> Lua.StackIndex -> Lua.LuaE e (Either Text FilePath)
getPathArg config idx = do
    bs <- Lua.tostring' idx
    let path = Text.unpack $ Text.decodeUtf8 bs
    pure $ validatePath config path

-- | Push a list of strings as a Lua table.
pushStringList :: [String] -> Lua.LuaE e ()
pushStringList items = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(i, s) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack s)
        Lua.seti (Lua.nthFromTop 2) (fromIntegral i)
        ) (zip [1..] items)
"}

contents.userPrompt:
You are an expert Haskell and Bash programmer specialized in writing clean, idiomatic, and efficient Haskell code.
When writing Bash code:
- Avoid bash-specific logic but bash-ism are fine.
- Document required shell tools at the top of the bash script.
When writing Haskell code:
- Use modern Haskell practices (base >= 4.14)
- Prefer pure functions and explicit type signatures
- Use standard libraries when appropriate
- Add clear comments for complex logic
- Follow consistent formatting
Use list-files to explore the project structure, read-file to understand existing code, write-file to create or modify files, and check-code to verify your work.
Always compile code before considering the job done.
Your output format may be stored as commit message: hence the first line of your last message should be a summary line.

function.arguments:
{"filepath": "./src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox/Modules/Time.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}

{- | Time module for LuaToolbox.

Provides time-related functions:

> local time = require("time")
> local now = time.now()                          -- timestamp (seconds since epoch)
> time.sleep(2)                                   -- sleep 2 seconds
> local formatted = time.format(now, "%Y-%m-%d")  -- "2024-01-15"
> local diff = time.diff(t1, t2)                  -- difference in seconds

Time values are represented as seconds since Unix epoch (January 1, 1970 UTC),
as floating point numbers for sub-second precision.
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Time (
    registerTimeModule,
) where

import Control.Concurrent (threadDelay)
import Control.Monad.IO.Class (liftIO)
import Data.Time (UTCTime, defaultTimeLocale, formatTime, getCurrentTime, diffUTCTime)
import Data.Time.Clock.POSIX (utcTimeToPOSIXSeconds, posixSecondsToUTCTime)
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified HsLua as Lua

-- | Register the time module in the Lua state.
registerTimeModule :: Lua.State -> IO ()
registerTimeModule lstate = Lua.runWith lstate $ do
    Lua.newtable

    addFunction "now" luaNow
    addFunction "sleep" luaSleep
    addFunction "format" luaFormat
    addFunction "diff" luaDiff

    Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "time")
  where
    addFunction :: BS.ByteString -> Lua.HsFn e -> Lua.LuaE e ()
    addFunction name fn = do
        Lua.pushName (Lua.Name name)
        Lua.pushhsfunction fn
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthFromTop 3)

import qualified Data.ByteString as BS

-- | Get current timestamp.
-- Lua: time.now() -> timestamp (seconds since epoch)
luaNow :: Lua.HsFn e
luaNow = do
    now <- liftIO getCurrentTime
    let seconds = realToFrac $ utcTimeToPOSIXSeconds now :: Double
    Lua.pushnumber seconds
    pure 1

-- | Sleep for specified seconds.
-- Lua: time.sleep(seconds)
luaSleep :: Lua.HsFn e
luaSleep = do
    mSeconds <- Lua.tonumber Lua.stackTop
    Lua.pop 1
    case mSeconds of

You are an expert Haskell and Bash programmer specialized in writing clean, idiomatic, and efficient Haskell code.
When writing Bash code:
- Avoid bash-specific logic but bash-ism are fine.
- Document required shell tools at the top of the bash script.
When writing Haskell code:
- Use modern Haskell practices (base >= 4.14)
- Prefer pure functions and explicit type signatures
- Use standard libraries when appropriate
- Add clear comments for complex logic
- Follow consistent formatting
Use list-files to explore the project structure, read-file to understand existing code, write-file to create or modify files, and check-code to verify your work.
Always compile code before considering the job done.
Your output format may be stored as commit message: hence the first line of your last message should be a summary line.

function.arguments:
{"filepath": "./src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox/Modules/Text.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}

{- | UTF-8 text utilities module for LuaToolbox.

Provides string manipulation functions with proper UTF-8 support:

> local text = require("text")
> text.split("a,b,c", ",")           -- {"a", "b", "c"}
> text.find("hello world", "world")  -- 7, 11 (start, end positions)
> text.gsub("hello", "l", "x")       -- "hexxo", 2
> text.trim("  hello  ")              -- "hello"
> text.startswith("hello", "he")     -- true
> text.endswith("hello", "lo")       -- true
> text.lower("HELLO")                -- "hello"
> text.upper("hello")                -- "HELLO"
> text.len("hello")                  -- 5
> text.sub("hello", 2, 4)            -- "ell"

All operations work correctly with non-ASCII Unicode characters.
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Text (
    registerTextModule,
) where

import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified HsLua as Lua

-- | Register the text module in the Lua state.
registerTextModule :: Lua.State -> IO ()
registerTextModule lstate = Lua.runWith lstate $ do
    Lua.newtable

    addFunction "split" luaSplit
    addFunction "find" luaFind
    addFunction "gsub" luaGsub
    addFunction "trim" luaTrim
    addFunction "startswith" luaStartswith
    addFunction "endswith" luaEndswith
    addFunction "lower" luaLower
    addFunction "upper" luaUpper
    addFunction "len" luaLen
    addFunction "sub" luaSub

    Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "text")
  where
    addFunction :: BS.ByteString -> Lua.HsFn e -> Lua.LuaE e ()
    addFunction name fn = do
        Lua.pushName (Lua.Name name)
        Lua.pushhsfunction fn
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthFromTop 3)

import qualified Data.ByteString as BS

-- | Split a string by a delimiter.
-- Lua: text.split(str, delimiter) -> {parts...}
luaSplit :: Lua.HsFn e
luaSplit = do
    str <- getTextArg 1
    delim <- getTextArg 2
    Lua.pop 2

    let parts = Text.splitOn delim str
    pushTextList parts
    pure 1

-- | Find substring position.
-- Lua: text.find(str, pattern) -> start, end or nil
luaFind :: Lua.HsFn e
luaFind = do
    str <- getTextArg 1
    pattern <- getTextArg 2
    Lua.pop 2

    case Text.breakOn pattern str of
        (before, "") -> do
            -- Pattern not found
            Lua.pushnil
            pure 1
        (before, _) -> do
            let startPos = Text.length before + 1  -- 1-indexed
            let endPos = startPos + Text.length pattern - 1
            Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral startPos)
            Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral endPos)
            pure 2

-- | Global substitution (simple string replacement, not pattern matching).
-- Lua: text.gsub(str, pattern, replacement) -> new_str, count
luaGsub :: Lua.HsFn e
luaGsub = do
    str <- getTextArg 1
    pattern <- getTextArg 2
    replacement <- getTextArg 3
    Lua.pop 3

    let parts = Text.splitOn pattern str
    let count = length parts - 1
    let result = Text.intercalate replacement parts
    
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 result)
    Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral count)
    pure 2

-- | Trim whitespace from both ends.
-- Lua: text.trim(str) -> trimmed_str
luaTrim :: Lua.HsFn e
luaTrim = do
    str <- getTextArg 1
    Lua.pop 1
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.strip str)
    pure 1

-- | Check if string starts with prefix.
-- Lua: text.startswith(str, prefix) -> boolean
luaStartswith :: Lua.HsFn e
luaStartswith = do
    str <- getTextArg 1
    prefix <- getTextArg 2
    Lua.pop 2
    Lua.pushboolean (prefix `Text.isPrefixOf` str)
    pure 1

-- | Check if string ends with suffix.
-- Lua: text.endswith(str, suffix) -> boolean
luaEndswith :: Lua.HsFn e
luaEndswith = do
    str <- getTextArg 1
    suffix <- getTextArg 2
    Lua.pop 2
    Lua.pushboolean (suffix `Text.isSuffixOf` str)
    pure 1

-- | Convert to lowercase.
-- Lua: text.lower(str) -> lower_str
luaLower :: Lua.HsFn e
luaLower = do
    str <- getTextArg 1
    Lua.pop 1
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.toLower str)
    pure 1

-- | Convert to uppercase.
-- Lua: text.upper(str) -> upper_str
luaUpper :: Lua.HsFn e
luaUpper = do
    str <- getTextArg 1
    Lua.pop 1
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.toUpper str)
    pure 1

-- | Get string length (in characters, not bytes).
-- Lua: text.len(str) -> length
luaLen :: Lua.HsFn e
luaLen = do
    str <- getTextArg 1
    Lua.pop 1
    Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral $ Text.length str)
    pure 1

-- | Get substring (1-indexed, inclusive).
-- Lua: text.sub(str, start, end) -> substring
-- If end is omitted, goes to end of string.
luaSub :: Lua.HsFn e
luaSub = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop
    str <- getTextArg 1
    mStart <- Lua.tointeger (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
    Lua.pop nargs

    case mStart of
        Nothing -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring "Invalid start index"
            pure 2
        Just startIdx -> do
            let len = Text.length str
            let start = fromIntegral startIdx - 1  -- Convert to 0-indexed
            
            -- Handle negative indices
            let actualStart = if start < 0 then len + start + 1 else start
            
            if actualStart < 0 || actualStart >= len
                then do
                    Lua.pushstring ""
                    pure 1
                else do
                    let result = Text.drop actualStart str
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 result)
                    pure 1

-- | Helper to get a text argument from the stack.
getTextArg :: Lua.StackIndex -> Lua.LuaE e Text
getTextArg idx = do
    bs <- Lua.tostring' idx
    pure $ Text.decodeUtf8 bs

-- | Push a list of Text values as a Lua table.
pushTextList :: [Text] -> Lua.LuaE e ()
pushTextList items = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(i, txt) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 txt)
        Lua.seti (Lua.nthFromTop 2) (fromIntegral i)
        ) (zip [1..] items)
"}

    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.toLower str)
    pure 1

-- | Convert to uppercase.
-- Lua: text.upper(str) -> upper_str
luaUpper :: Lua.HsFn e
luaUpper = do
    str <- getTextArg 1
    Lua.pop 1
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.toUpper str)
    pure 1

-- | Get string length (in characters, not bytes).
-- Lua: text.len(str) -> length
luaLen :: Lua.HsFn e
luaLen = do
    str <- getTextArg 1
    Lua.pop 1
    Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral $ Text.length str)
    pure 1

-- | Get substring (1-indexed, inclusive).
-- Lua: text.sub(str, start, end) -> substring
-- If end is omitted, goes to end of string.
luaSub :: Lua.HsFn e
luaSub = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop
    str <- getTextArg 1
    mStart <- Lua.tointeger (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
    Lua.pop nargs

    case mStart of
        Nothing -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring "Invalid start index"
            pure 2
        Just startIdx -> do
            let len = Text.length str
            let start = fromIntegral startIdx - 1  -- Convert to 0-indexed
            
            -- Handle negative indices
            let actualStart = if start < 0 then len + start + 1 else start
            
            if actualStart < 0 || actualStart >= len
                then do
                    Lua.pushstring ""
                    pure 1
                else do
                    let result = Text.drop actualStart str
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 result)
                    pure 1

-- | Helper to get a text argument from the stack.
getTextArg :: Lua.StackIndex -> Lua.LuaE e Text
getTextArg idx = do
    bs <- Lua.tostring' idx
    pure $ Text.decodeUtf8 bs

-- | Push a list of Text values as a Lua table.
pushTextList :: [Text] -> Lua.LuaE e ()
pushTextList items = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(i, txt) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 txt)
        Lua.seti (Lua.nthFromTop 2) (fromIntegral i)
        ) (zip [1..] items)
"}

{- | UTF-8 text utilities module for LuaToolbox.

Provides string manipulation functions with proper UTF-8 support:

> local text = require("text")
> text.split("a,b,c", ",")           -- {"a", "b", "c"}
> text.find("hello world", "world")  -- 7, 11 (start, end positions)
> text.gsub("hello", "l", "x")       -- "hexxo", 2
> text.trim("  hello  ")              -- "hello"
> text.startswith("hello", "he")     -- true
> text.endswith("hello", "lo")       -- true
> text.lower("HELLO")                -- "hello"
> text.upper("hello")                -- "HELLO"
> text.len("hello")                  -- 5
> text.sub("hello", 2, 4)            -- "ell"

All operations work correctly with non-ASCII Unicode characters.
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Text (
    registerTextModule,
) where

import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified HsLua as Lua

-- | Register the text module in the Lua state.
registerTextModule :: Lua.State -> IO ()
registerTextModule lstate = Lua.runWith lstate $ do
    Lua.newtable

    addFunction "split" luaSplit
    addFunction "find" luaFind
    addFunction "gsub" luaGsub
    addFunction "trim" luaTrim
    addFunction "startswith" luaStartswith
    addFunction "endswith" luaEndswith
    addFunction "lower" luaLower
    addFunction "upper" luaUpper
    addFunction "len" luaLen
    addFunction "sub" luaSub

    Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "text")
  where
    addFunction :: BS.ByteString -> Lua.HsFn e -> Lua.LuaE e ()
    addFunction name fn = do
        Lua.pushName (Lua.Name name)
        Lua.pushhsfunction fn
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthFromTop 3)

import qualified Data.ByteString as BS

-- | Split a string by a delimiter.
-- Lua: text.split(str, delimiter) -> {parts...}
luaSplit :: Lua.HsFn e
luaSplit = do
    str <- getTextArg 1
    delim <- getTextArg 2
    Lua.pop 2

    let parts = Text.splitOn delim str
    pushTextList parts
    pure 1

-- | Find substring position.
-- Lua: text.find(str, pattern) -> start, end or nil
luaFind :: Lua.HsFn e
luaFind = do
    str <- getTextArg 1
    pattern <- getTextArg 2
    Lua.pop 2

    case Text.breakOn pattern str of
        (before, "") -> do
            -- Pattern not found
            Lua.pushnil
            pure 1
        (before, _) -> do
            let startPos = Text.length before + 1  -- 1-indexed
            let endPos = startPos + Text.length pattern - 1
            Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral startPos)
            Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral endPos)
            pure 2

-- | Global substitution (simple string replacement, not pattern matching).
-- Lua: text.gsub(str, pattern, replacement) -> new_str, count
luaGsub :: Lua.HsFn e
luaGsub = do
    str <- getTextArg 1
    pattern <- getTextArg 2
    replacement <- getTextArg 3
    Lua.pop 3

    let parts = Text.splitOn pattern str
    let count = length parts - 1
    let result = Text.intercalate replacement parts
    
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 result)
    Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral count)
    pure 2

-- | Trim whitespace from both ends.
-- Lua: text.trim(str) -> trimmed_str
luaTrim :: Lua.HsFn e
luaTrim = do
    str <- getTextArg 1
    Lua.pop 1
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.strip str)
    pure 1

-- | Check if string starts with prefix.
-- Lua: text.startswith(str, prefix) -> boolean
luaStartswith :: Lua.HsFn e
luaStartswith = do
    str <- getTextArg 1
    prefix <- getTextArg 2
    Lua.pop 2
    Lua.pushboolean (prefix `Text.isPrefixOf` str)
    pure 1

-- | Check if string ends with suffix.
-- Lua: text.endswith(str, suffix) -> boolean
luaEndswith :: Lua.HsFn e
luaEndswith = do
    str <- getTextArg 1
    suffix <- getTextArg 2
    Lua.pop 2
    Lua.pushboolean (suffix `Text.isSuffixOf` str)
    pure 1

-- | Convert to lowercase.
-- Lua: text.lower(str) -> lower_str
luaLower :: Lua.HsFn e
luaLower = do
    str <- getTextArg 1
    Lua.pop 1
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.toLower str)
    pure 1

-- | Convert to uppercase.
-- Lua: text.upper(str) -> upper_str
luaUpper :: Lua.HsFn e
luaUpper = do
    str <- getTextArg 1
    Lua.pop 1
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.toUpper str)
    pure 1

-- | Get string length (in characters, not bytes).
-- Lua: text.len(str) -> length
luaLen :: Lua.HsFn e
luaLen = do
    str <- getTextArg 1
    Lua.pop 1
    Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral $ Text.length str)
    pure 1

-- | Get substring (1-indexed, inclusive).
-- Lua: text.sub(str, start, end) -> substring
-- If end is omitted, goes to end of string.
luaSub :: Lua.HsFn e
luaSub = do
    nargs <- Lua.gettop

    Lua.pop nargs

    case mStart of
        Nothing -> do
            Lua.pushnil

            pure 2
        Just startIdx -> do
            let len = Text.length str
            let start = fromIntegral startIdx - 1  -- Convert to 0-indexed
            
            -- Handle negative indices
            let actualStart = if start < 0 then len + start + 1 else start
            
            if actualStart < 0 || actualStart >= len
                then do
                    Lua.pushstring ""
                    pure 1
                else do
                    let result = Text.drop actualStart str
                    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 result)
                    pure 1

-- | Helper to get a text argument from the stack.
getTextArg :: Lua.StackIndex -> Lua.LuaE e Text
getTextArg idx = do
    bs <- Lua.tostring' idx
    pure $ Text.decodeUtf8 bs

-- | Push a list of Text values as a Lua table.
pushTextList :: [Text] -> Lua.LuaE e ()

function.arguments:
{"filepath": "./src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox/Modules/Json.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}

{- | JSON module for LuaToolbox.

Provides JSON encoding and decoding functions to Lua scripts:

> local json = require("json")
> local encoded = json.encode({foo = "bar", count = 42})
> -- encoded: '{"foo":"bar","count":42}'
> local decoded = json.decode('{"foo":"bar"}')
> -- decoded: {foo = "bar"}

Implementation notes:
* JSON numbers become Lua numbers (doubles). Large integers may lose precision.
* JSON null becomes Lua nil.
* JSON arrays become Lua tables with integer keys starting at 1.
* JSON objects become Lua tables with string keys.
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Json (
    registerJsonModule,
) where

import qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson
import qualified Data.Aeson.Text as AesonText
import qualified Data.ByteString as BS
import Data.Scientific (Scientific, floatingOrInteger)
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Lazy as LazyText
import qualified Data.Vector as Vector
import qualified HsLua as Lua

import qualified Data.Aeson.KeyMap as KeyMap
import qualified Data.Aeson.Key as AesonKey

-- | Register the json module in the Lua state.
registerJsonModule :: Lua.State -> IO ()
registerJsonModule lstate = Lua.runWith lstate $ do
    Lua.newtable  -- Create module table

    -- json.encode(value) -> string
    Lua.pushName "encode"
    Lua.pushhsfunction luaEncode
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthFromTop 3)

    -- json.decode(string) -> value
    Lua.pushName "decode"
    Lua.pushhsfunction luaDecode
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthFromTop 3)

    -- Register as 'json' module
    Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "json")

-- | Encode a Lua value to JSON string.
-- Lua: json.encode(value) -> string
luaEncode :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaEncode = do
    -- Convert Lua value to Aeson Value, then to string
    jsonVal <- luaToAesonValue
    let txt = LazyText.toStrict $ AesonText.encodeToLazyText jsonVal
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 txt)
    pure 1

-- | Decode a JSON string to a Lua value.
-- Lua: json.decode(string) -> value or nil, error
luaDecode :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaDecode = do
    -- Get string argument
    str <- Lua.tostring' Lua.stackTop
    Lua.pop 1
    case Aeson.eitherDecodeStrict' str of
        Left err -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack err)
            pure 2  -- nil, error
        Right val -> do
            aesonToLuaValue val
            pure 1

-- | Convert Lua value at top of stack to Aeson Value.
luaToAesonValue :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
luaToAesonValue = do
    ltype <- Lua.ltype Lua.stackTop
    case ltype of
        Lua.TypeNil -> do
            Lua.pop 1
            pure Aeson.Null
        Lua.TypeBoolean -> do
            b <- Lua.toboolean Lua.stackTop
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.Bool b
        Lua.TypeNumber -> do
            mNum <- Lua.tonumber Lua.stackTop
            Lua.pop 1
            case mNum of
                Nothing -> pure Aeson.Null
                Just n -> pure $ Aeson.Number $ fromFloatDigits n
        Lua.TypeString -> do
            s <- Lua.tostring' Lua.stackTop
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.String (Text.decodeUtf8 s)
        Lua.TypeTable -> do
            convertTable
        _ -> do
            -- For other types, convert to string
            str <- luaValueToString
            pure $ Aeson.String str
  where
    fromFloatDigits :: Double -> Scientific
    fromFloatDigits = read . show

    luaValueToString :: Lua.LuaE e Text
    luaValueToString = do
        Lua.pushglobaltable
        Lua.pushName (Lua.Name "tostring")
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
        _ <- Lua.pcall 1 1 Nothing
        s <- Lua.tostring' Lua.stackTop
        Lua.pop 1
        pure $ Text.decodeUtf8 s

-- | Convert Lua table to Aeson Value (object or array).
convertTable :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertTable = do
    isArr <- isArray
    if isArr
        then convertArray
        else convertObject

-- | Check if table at top of stack is an array.
-- An array has sequential integer keys starting at 1.
isArray :: Lua.LuaE e Bool
isArray = do
    Lua.pushnil
    isSequential 1
  where
    isSequential :: Int -> Lua.LuaE e Bool
    isSequential expectedIdx = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then pure True
            else do
                isNum <- Lua.isnumber (Lua.nthFromTop 1)
                if isNum
                    then do
                        mIdx <- Lua.tointeger (Lua.nthFromTop 1)
                        Lua.pop 2
                        case mIdx of
                            Just idx
                                | idx == fromIntegral expectedIdx ->
                                    isSequential (expectedIdx + 1)
                            _ -> pure False
                    else do
                        Lua.pop 2
                        pure False

-- | Convert array table to JSON Array.
convertArray :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertArray = do
    vals <- collectArrayValues
    pure $ Aeson.Array (Vector.fromList vals)

-- | Collect values from array table.
collectArrayValues :: Lua.LuaE e [Aeson.Value]
collectArrayValues = do
    len' <- Lua.rawlen Lua.stackTop
    vals <- mapM (\i -> do
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
        val <- luaToAesonValue
        pure val
        ) [1 .. fromIntegral len']
    Lua.pop 1  -- pop the table
    pure vals

-- | Convert object table to JSON Object.
convertObject :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertObject = do
    pairs <- collectObjectPairs
    pure $ Aeson.Object (KeyMap.fromList pairs)

-- | Collect key-value pairs from object table.
collectObjectPairs :: Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
collectObjectPairs = do
    Lua.pushnil
    go []
  where
    go :: [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)] -> Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
    go acc = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then do
                Lua.pop 1  -- pop the table
                pure acc
            else do
                key <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
                val <- luaToAesonValue
                Lua.pop 1  -- pop key
                go ((AesonKey.fromText (Text.decodeUtf8 key), val) : acc)

-- | Convert Aeson Value to Lua value and push onto stack.
aesonToLuaValue :: Aeson.Value -> Lua.LuaE e ()
aesonToLuaValue Aeson.Null = Lua.pushnil
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Bool b) = Lua.pushboolean b
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Number n) = 
    case floatingOrInteger n of
        Left d -> Lua.pushnumber d
        Right i -> Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.String t) = Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 t)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Array arr) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(i, val) -> do
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.seti (Lua.nthFromTop 2) (fromIntegral (i + 1))  -- Lua arrays are 1-indexed
        ) (zip [0..] (Vector.toList arr))
    -- Replace nil key with the table
    return ()
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Object obj) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(k, val) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ AesonKey.toText k)
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthFromTop 3)
        ) (KeyMap.toList obj)
"}

-- | Collect key-value pairs from object table.
collectObjectPairs :: Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
collectObjectPairs = do
    Lua.pushnil
    go []
  where
    go :: [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)] -> Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
    go acc = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then do
                Lua.pop 1  -- pop the table
                pure acc
            else do
                key <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
                val <- luaToAesonValue
                Lua.pop 1  -- pop key
                go ((AesonKey.fromText (Text.decodeUtf8 key), val) : acc)

-- | Convert Aeson Value to Lua value and push onto stack.
aesonToLuaValue :: Aeson.Value -> Lua.LuaE e ()
aesonToLuaValue Aeson.Null = Lua.pushnil
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Bool b) = Lua.pushboolean b
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Number n) = 
    case floatingOrInteger n of
        Left d -> Lua.pushnumber d
        Right i -> Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.String t) = Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 t)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Array arr) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(i, val) -> do
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.seti (Lua.nthFromTop 2) (fromIntegral (i + 1))  -- Lua arrays are 1-indexed
        ) (zip [0..] (Vector.toList arr))
    -- Replace nil key with the table
    return ()
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Object obj) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(k, val) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ AesonKey.toText k)
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthFromTop 3)
        ) (KeyMap.toList obj)
"}

{"filepath": "./src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox/Modules/Json.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}

{- | JSON module for LuaToolbox.

Provides JSON encoding and decoding functions to Lua scripts:

> local json = require("json")
> local encoded = json.encode({foo = "bar", count = 42})
> -- encoded: '{"foo":"bar","count":42}'
> local decoded = json.decode('{"foo":"bar"}')
> -- decoded: {foo = "bar"}

Implementation notes:
* JSON numbers become Lua numbers (doubles). Large integers may lose precision.
* JSON null becomes Lua nil.
* JSON arrays become Lua tables with integer keys starting at 1.
* JSON objects become Lua tables with string keys.
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Json (
    registerJsonModule,
) where

import qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson
import qualified Data.Aeson.Text as AesonText
import qualified Data.ByteString as BS
import Data.Scientific (Scientific, floatingOrInteger)
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Lazy as LazyText
import qualified Data.Vector as Vector
import qualified HsLua as Lua

import qualified Data.Aeson.KeyMap as KeyMap
import qualified Data.Aeson.Key as AesonKey

-- | Register the json module in the Lua state.
registerJsonModule :: Lua.State -> IO ()
registerJsonModule lstate = Lua.runWith lstate $ do
    Lua.newtable  -- Create module table

    -- json.encode(value) -> string
    Lua.pushName "encode"
    Lua.pushhsfunction luaEncode
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthFromTop 3)

    -- json.decode(string) -> value
    Lua.pushName "decode"
    Lua.pushhsfunction luaDecode
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthFromTop 3)

    -- Register as 'json' module
    Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "json")

-- | Encode a Lua value to JSON string.
-- Lua: json.encode(value) -> string
luaEncode :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaEncode = do
    -- Convert Lua value to Aeson Value, then to string
    jsonVal <- luaToAesonValue
    let txt = LazyText.toStrict $ AesonText.encodeToLazyText jsonVal
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 txt)
    pure 1

-- | Decode a JSON string to a Lua value.
-- Lua: json.decode(string) -> value or nil, error
luaDecode :: Lua.LuaE e Lua.NumResults
luaDecode = do
    -- Get string argument
    str <- Lua.tostring' Lua.stackTop
    Lua.pop 1
    case Aeson.eitherDecodeStrict' str of
        Left err -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack err)
            pure 2  -- nil, error
        Right val -> do
            aesonToLuaValue val
            pure 1

-- | Convert Lua value at top of stack to Aeson Value.
luaToAesonValue :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
luaToAesonValue = do
    ltype <- Lua.ltype Lua.stackTop
    case ltype of
        Lua.TypeNil -> do
            Lua.pop 1
            pure Aeson.Null
        Lua.TypeBoolean -> do
            b <- Lua.toboolean Lua.stackTop
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.Bool b
        Lua.TypeNumber -> do
            mNum <- Lua.tonumber Lua.stackTop
            Lua.pop 1
            case mNum of
                Nothing -> pure Aeson.Null
                Just n -> pure $ Aeson.Number $ fromFloatDigits n
        Lua.TypeString -> do
            s <- Lua.tostring' Lua.stackTop
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.String (Text.decodeUtf8 s)
        Lua.TypeTable -> do
            convertTable
        _ -> do
            -- For other types, convert to string
            str <- luaValueToString
            pure $ Aeson.String str
  where
    fromFloatDigits :: Double -> Scientific
    fromFloatDigits = read . show

    luaValueToString :: Lua.LuaE e Text
    luaValueToString = do
        Lua.pushglobaltable
        Lua.pushName (Lua.Name "tostring")
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
        _ <- Lua.pcall 1 1 Nothing
        s <- Lua.tostring' Lua.stackTop
        Lua.pop 1
        pure $ Text.decodeUtf8 s

-- | Convert Lua table to Aeson Value (object or array).
convertTable :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertTable = do
    isArr <- isArray
    if isArr
        then convertArray
        else convertObject

-- | Check if table at top of stack is an array.
-- An array has sequential integer keys starting at 1.
isArray :: Lua.LuaE e Bool
isArray = do
    Lua.pushnil
    isSequential 1
  where
    isSequential :: Int -> Lua.LuaE e Bool
    isSequential expectedIdx = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then pure True
            else do
                isNum <- Lua.isnumber (Lua.nthFromTop 1)
                if isNum
                    then do
                        mIdx <- Lua.tointeger (Lua.nthFromTop 1)
                        Lua.pop 2
                        case mIdx of
                            Just idx
                                | idx == fromIntegral expectedIdx ->
                                    isSequential (expectedIdx + 1)
                            _ -> pure False
                    else do
                        Lua.pop 2
                        pure False

-- | Convert array table to JSON Array.
convertArray :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertArray = do
    vals <- collectArrayValues
    pure $ Aeson.Array (Vector.fromList vals)

-- | Collect values from array table.
collectArrayValues :: Lua.LuaE e [Aeson.Value]
collectArrayValues = do
    len' <- Lua.rawlen Lua.stackTop
    vals <- mapM (\i -> do
        Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
        val <- luaToAesonValue
        pure val
        ) [1 .. fromIntegral len']
    Lua.pop 1  -- pop the table
    pure vals

-- | Convert object table to JSON Object.
convertObject :: Lua.LuaE e Aeson.Value
convertObject = do
    pairs <- collectObjectPairs
    pure $ Aeson.Object (KeyMap.fromList pairs)

-- | Collect key-value pairs from object table.
collectObjectPairs :: Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
collectObjectPairs = do
    Lua.pushnil
    go []
  where
    go :: [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)] -> Lua.LuaE e [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
    go acc = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then do
                Lua.pop 1  -- pop the table
                pure acc
            else do
                key <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthFromTop 2)
                val <- luaToAesonValue
                Lua.pop 1  -- pop key
                go ((AesonKey.fromText (Text.decodeUtf8 key), val) : acc)

-- | Convert Aeson Value to Lua value and push onto stack.
aesonToLuaValue :: Aeson.Value -> Lua.LuaE e ()
aesonToLuaValue Aeson.Null = Lua.pushnil
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Bool b) = Lua.pushboolean b
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Number n) = 
    case floatingOrInteger n of
        Left d -> Lua.pushnumber d
        Right i -> Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.String t) = Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 t)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Array arr) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(i, val) -> do
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.seti (Lua.nthFromTop 2) (fromIntegral (i + 1))  -- Lua arrays are 1-indexed
        ) (zip [0..] (Vector.toList arr))
    -- Replace nil key with the table
    return ()
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Object obj) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_ (\(k, val) -> do
        Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ AesonKey.toText k)
        aesonToLuaValue val
        Lua.settable (Lua.nthFromTop 3)
        ) (KeyMap.toList obj)
"}
